 147 |     annotations: list[ast.AST] = []
 148 |     args = node.args
 149 |     for arg in [*args.posonlyargs, *args.args, *args.kwonlyargs]:
 150 |         if arg.annotation is not None:
 151 |             annotations.append(arg.annotation)
 152 |     if args.vararg is not None and args.vararg.annotation is not None:
 153 |         annotations.append(args.vararg.annotation)
 154 |     if args.kwarg is not None and args.kwarg.annotation is not None:
 155 |         annotations.append(args.kwarg.annotation)
 156 |     if node.returns is not None:
 157 |         annotations.append(node.returns)
 158 |     return annotations
 159 | 
 160 | 
 161 | def _validate_top_level_function(node: ast.FunctionDef, *, postponed_annotations: bool) -> None:
 162 |     if node.name in _PROTECTED_NAMES:
 163 |         raise ValueError(
 164 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: top-level function '{node.name}' "
 165 |             "shadows a protected scorer or harness name."
 166 |         )
 167 |     if node.decorator_list:
 168 |         raise ValueError(
 169 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: top-level function decorators are not "
 170 |             "allowed because decorators execute during candidate import."
 171 |         )
 172 |     if node.args.defaults or node.args.kw_defaults:
 173 |         raise ValueError(
 174 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: top-level function default arguments "
 175 |             "are not allowed because defaults execute during candidate import."
 176 |         )
 177 |     if _function_annotations(node) and not postponed_annotations:
 178 |         raise ValueError(
 179 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: top-level function annotations require "
 180 |             "'from __future__ import annotations' because annotations otherwise "
 181 |             "execute during candidate import."
 182 |         )
 183 | 
 184 | 
 185 | def _call_name(func: ast.AST) -> str:
 186 |     if isinstance(func, ast.Name):
 187 |         return func.id
 188 |     if isinstance(func, ast.Attribute) and isinstance(func.value, ast.Name):
 189 |         return f"{func.value.id}.{func.attr}"
 190 |     return ""
 191 | 
 192 | 
 193 | def _assigned_names(target: ast.AST) -> set[str]:
 194 |     if isinstance(target, ast.Name):
 195 |         return {target.id}
 196 |     if isinstance(target, (ast.Tuple, ast.List)):
 197 |         names: set[str] = set()
 198 |         for elt in target.elts:
 199 |             names.update(_assigned_names(elt))
 200 |         return names
 201 |     return set()
 202 | 
 203 | 
 204 | def _validate_plain_import(alias: ast.alias) -> None:
 205 |     if alias.name not in _ALLOWED_IMPORTS:
 206 |         raise ValueError(
 207 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: import '{alias.name}' is not allowed. "
 208 |             "Candidates may only import sympy/numpy and the equation harness."
 209 |         )
 210 |     if alias.name == "sympy" and alias.asname != "sp":
 211 |         raise ValueError(
 212 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: sympy must be imported canonically "
 213 |             "as 'import sympy as sp'."
 214 |         )
 215 |     if alias.name == "numpy" and alias.asname not in {None, "np"}:
 216 |         raise ValueError(
 217 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: numpy may only be imported as "
 218 |             "'import numpy' or 'import numpy as np'."
 219 |         )
 220 | 
 221 | 
 222 | def _validate_evaluate_expression_data_args(call: ast.Call) -> set[int]:
 223 |     expected = ["X_train", "y_train", "X_val", "y_val"]
 224 |     if len(call.args) < 5:
 225 |         raise ValueError(
 226 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: evaluate_expression() must be called "
 227 |             "as evaluate_expression(expression, X_train, y_train, X_val, y_val, ...)."
 228 |         )
 229 |     actual_data_args = call.args[1:5]
 230 |     if not all(
 231 |         isinstance(arg, ast.Name) and arg.id == expected_name
 232 |         for arg, expected_name in zip(actual_data_args, expected)
 233 |     ):
 234 |         raise ValueError(
 235 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: evaluate_expression() must use the "
 236 |             "canonical data arguments exactly as X_train, y_train, X_val, y_val. "
 237 |             "Do not fit constants on validation labels or reorder the split."
 238 |         )
 239 |     return {id(arg) for arg in call.args[1:5]}
 240 | 
 241 | 
 242 | def _x_train_shape_feature_arg_id(node: ast.AST) -> int | None:
 243 |     if not (
 244 |         isinstance(node, ast.Subscript)
 245 |         and isinstance(node.value, ast.Attribute)
 246 |         and node.value.attr == "shape"
 247 |         and isinstance(node.value.value, ast.Name)
 248 |         and node.value.value.id == "X_train"
 249 |     ):
 250 |         return None
 251 |     slice_node = node.slice
 252 |     if isinstance(slice_node, ast.Constant) and slice_node.value == 1:
 253 |         return id(node.value.value)
 254 |     return None
 255 | 
 256 | 
 257 | def _allowed_feature_symbols_data_ids(fn: ast.FunctionDef) -> set[int]:
 258 |     allowed: set[int] = set()
 259 |     for node in ast.walk(fn):
 260 |         if not (
 261 |             isinstance(node, ast.Call)
 262 |             and isinstance(node.func, ast.Name)
 263 |             and node.func.id == "feature_symbols"
 264 |             and len(node.args) == 1
 265 |         ):
 266 |             continue
 267 |         x_train_id = _x_train_shape_feature_arg_id(node.args[0])
 268 |         if x_train_id is not None:
 269 |             allowed.add(x_train_id)
 270 |     return allowed
 271 | 
 272 | 
 273 | def _validate_no_dataset_data_leak(fn: ast.FunctionDef, allowed_node_ids: set[int]) -> None:
 274 |     for node in ast.walk(fn):
 275 |         if (
 276 |             isinstance(node, ast.Name)
 277 |             and node.id in {"X_train", "y_train", "X_val", "y_val"}
 278 |             and id(node) not in allowed_node_ids
 279 |         ):
 280 |             raise ValueError(
 281 |                 f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: dataset array '{node.id}' may only "
 282 |                 "appear in feature_symbols(X_train.shape[1]) or as the canonical "
 283 |                 "evaluate_expression(expression, X_train, y_train, X_val, y_val, ...) "
 284 |                 "arguments. Do not use dataset values to choose expressions, mutate "
 285 |                 "splits, or tune constants/initial values."
 286 |             )
 287 | 
 288 | 
 289 | def validate_candidate_source(program_path: str) -> None:
 290 |     """Reject reward-hacking program structure before importing candidate code."""
 291 |     source = Path(program_path).read_text()
 292 |     tree = ast.parse(source, filename=program_path)
 293 |     postponed_annotations = _has_postponed_annotations(tree)
 294 | 
 295 |     for node in tree.body:
 296 |         if _is_module_docstring(node) or _is_main_guard(node):
 297 |             continue
 298 |         if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef):
 299 |             _validate_top_level_function(node, postponed_annotations=postponed_annotations)
 300 |             continue
 301 |         if isinstance(node, ast.Import):
 302 |             for alias in node.names:
 303 |                 _validate_plain_import(alias)
 304 |         elif isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom):
 305 |             module = node.module or ""
 306 |             if module not in _ALLOWED_FROM_IMPORTS:
 307 |                 raise ValueError(
 308 |                     f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: from-import '{module}' is not allowed."
 309 |                 )
 310 |             if module == "pysr_harness.equation_session":
 311 |                 aliased = [alias.name for alias in node.names if alias.asname]
 312 |                 if aliased:
 313 |                     raise ValueError(
 314 |                         f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: harness imports may not "
 315 |                         f"use aliases: {sorted(aliased)}."
 316 |                     )
 317 |                 imported = {alias.name for alias in node.names}
 318 |                 extra = imported - _ALLOWED_HARNESS_IMPORTS
 319 |                 if extra:
 320 |                     raise ValueError(
 321 |                         f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: unsupported harness imports "
 322 |                         f"{sorted(extra)}. Use only feature_symbols, constant_symbols, "
 323 |                         "and evaluate_expression."
 324 |                     )
 325 |         else:
 326 |             raise ValueError(
 327 |                 f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: top-level executable code is not "
 328 |                 "allowed in candidate programs. Build and score the equation only "
 329 |                 "inside evaluate_symbolic_candidate()."
 330 |             )
 331 | 
 332 |     eval_fns = [
 333 |         node
 334 |         for node in tree.body
 335 |         if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef) and node.name == "evaluate_symbolic_candidate"
 336 |     ]
 337 |     if len(eval_fns) != 1:
 338 |         raise ValueError(
 339 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: program must define exactly one "
 340 |             "evaluate_symbolic_candidate() function."
 341 |         )
 342 |     fn = eval_fns[0]
 343 | 
 344 |     returns = [node for node in ast.walk(fn) if isinstance(node, ast.Return)]
 345 |     if len(returns) != 1:
 346 |         raise ValueError(
 347 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: evaluate_symbolic_candidate() must have "
 348 |             "exactly one return statement."
 349 |         )
 350 |     return_value = returns[0].value
 351 |     if not (
 352 |         isinstance(return_value, ast.Call)
 353 |         and isinstance(return_value.func, ast.Name)
 354 |         and return_value.func.id == "evaluate_expression"
 355 |     ):
 356 |         raise ValueError(
 357 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: evaluate_symbolic_candidate() must directly "
 358 |             "return evaluate_expression(...), with no wrapper or post-processing."
 359 |         )
 360 |     allowed_data_arg_ids = _validate_evaluate_expression_data_args(return_value)
 361 |     allowed_data_arg_ids.update(_allowed_feature_symbols_data_ids(fn))
 362 |     _validate_no_dataset_data_leak(fn, allowed_data_arg_ids)
 363 | 
 364 |     evaluate_calls = 0
 365 |     for node in ast.walk(fn):
 366 |         if isinstance(node, _DISALLOWED_CONTROL_NODES):
 367 |             raise ValueError(
 368 |                 f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: control flow, nested definitions, "
 369 |                 "comprehensions, and conditionals are not allowed inside "
 370 |                 "evaluate_symbolic_candidate(). Build one straight-line equation."
 371 |             )
 372 |         if isinstance(node, (ast.Import, ast.ImportFrom)):
 373 |             raise ValueError(
 374 |                 f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: imports are not allowed inside "
 375 |                 "evaluate_symbolic_candidate()."
 376 |             )
 377 |         if isinstance(node, (ast.Assign, ast.AnnAssign, ast.AugAssign)):
 378 |             targets = node.targets if isinstance(node, ast.Assign) else [node.target]
 379 |             assigned = set().union(*(_assigned_names(target) for target in targets))
 380 |             protected = assigned & _PROTECTED_NAMES
 381 |             if protected:
 382 |                 raise ValueError(
 383 |                     f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: cannot reassign protected scorer "
 384 |                     f"or harness names {sorted(protected)}."
 385 |                 )
 386 |         if isinstance(node, ast.Call):
 387 |             name = _call_name(node.func)
 388 |             if name == "evaluate_expression":
 389 |                 evaluate_calls += 1
 390 |             if name not in _ALLOWED_EVALUATE_CALLS:
 391 |                 raise ValueError(
 392 |                     f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: call '{name or type(node.func).__name__}' "
 393 |                     "is not allowed inside evaluate_symbolic_candidate(). Allowed calls "
 394 |                     "are the symbol helpers, evaluate_expression, and basic sp.* math."
 395 |                 )
 396 | 
 397 |     if evaluate_calls != 1:
 398 |         raise ValueError(
 399 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: evaluate_symbolic_candidate() must call "
 400 |             f"evaluate_expression() exactly once; found {evaluate_calls} calls."
 401 |         )
 402 | 
 403 | 
 404 | def _parse_parameter_from_column(column_name: str) -> float | None:
 405 |     """Extract a numeric parameter value from a fit.tsv column header.
 406 | 
 407 |     Handles diverse header formats found across disease-relevant datasets:
 408 |       - "... : 10uM"  or "10 uM"  → 10.0
 409 |       - "35uM" (bare, as in Abeta) → 35.0
 410 |       - "... : 3.95mM"             → 3950.0  (converted to uM)
 411 |       - "... : 0.3 mg/mL 22uM"    → 22.0    (prefer uM when both present)
 412 |       - "... : pH 2.7"             → 2.7
 413 |       - "... : 5% seeds"           → 5.0
 414 |       - "20 ng/ul"                 → 20.0
 415 |       - Non-numeric labels (WT, C19S, acid 10min, ...) → None
 416 |     """
 417 |     text = column_name.strip()
 418 | 
 419 |     # Prefer an explicit uM value
 420 |     m = re.search(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*uM", text, re.IGNORECASE)
 421 |     if m:
 422 |         return float(m.group(1))
 423 | 
 424 |     # mM → convert to uM
 425 |     m = re.search(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*mM", text, re.IGNORECASE)
 426 |     if m:
 427 |         return float(m.group(1)) * 1000.0
 428 | 
 429 |     # mg/mL (take the number)
 430 |     m = re.search(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*mg/mL", text, re.IGNORECASE)
 431 |     if m:
 432 |         return float(m.group(1))
 433 | 
 434 |     # ng/ul
 435 |     m = re.search(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*ng/ul", text, re.IGNORECASE)
 436 |     if m:
 437 |         return float(m.group(1))
 438 | 
 439 |     # pH value
 440 |     m = re.search(r"pH\s+(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)", text, re.IGNORECASE)
 441 |     if m:
 442 |         return float(m.group(1))
 443 | 
 444 |     # Percentage (e.g. "5% seeds")
 445 |     m = re.search(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*%", text)
 446 |     if m:
 447 |         return float(m.group(1))
 448 | 
 449 |     # Last resort: try to find any trailing number after colon/space
 450 |     m = re.search(r":\s*(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*$", text)
 451 |     if m:
 452 |         return float(m.group(1))
 453 | 
 454 |     return None
 455 | 
 456 | 
 457 | def _minmax_normalize(values: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
 458 |     """Normalize one dataset column to 0..1, preserving constants as zeros."""
 459 |     values = np.asarray(values, dtype=float)
 460 |     v_min = float(np.min(values))
 461 |     v_max = float(np.max(values))
 462 |     scale = v_max - v_min
 463 |     if scale <= 0.0:
 464 |         return np.zeros_like(values, dtype=float)
 465 |     return (values - v_min) / scale
 466 | 
 467 | 
 468 | def _load_fit_table(data_file: Path) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
 469 |     """Load a wide fit.tsv and return normalized X=[time, parameter, c], y.
 470 | 
 471 |     The first column is always the time/measurement coordinate. Remaining
 472 |     columns are response curves. The varying parameter (concentration, pH,
 473 |     etc.) is extracted from the header; if extraction fails, sequential
 474 |     indices 1, 2, 3, ... are used. Each dataset is then min-max normalized:
 475 |     time and parameter independently to 0..1, and the response globally across
 476 |     the whole fit.tsv to 0..1. Files already rescaled to 0..1 keep that scale.
 477 |     """
 478 |     with data_file.open(newline="") as f:
 479 |         reader = csv.reader(f, delimiter="\t")
 480 |         header = next(reader)
 481 | 
 482 |         response_headers = header[1:]
 483 |         parsed_params = [_parse_parameter_from_column(h) for h in response_headers]
 484 |         if all(p is not None for p in parsed_params):
 485 |             parameters = [float(p) for p in parsed_params]
 486 |         else:
 487 |             parameters = [float(i + 1) for i in range(len(response_headers))]
 488 | 
 489 |         features: list[list[float]] = []
 490 |         targets: list[float] = []
 491 |         for row in reader:
 492 |             if not row:
 493 |                 continue
 494 |             measurement_x = float(row[0])
 495 |             for param, value in zip(parameters, row[1:]):
 496 |                 if value == "" or value.strip().lower() == "nan":
 497 |                     continue
 498 |                 features.append([measurement_x, param])
 499 |                 targets.append(float(value))
 500 | 
 501 |     if not features:
 502 |         raise ValueError(f"No regression samples loaded from {data_file}")
 503 | 
 504 |     X = np.asarray(features, dtype=float)
 505 |     y = np.asarray(targets, dtype=float)
 506 |     X_norm = np.column_stack([_minmax_normalize(X[:, i]) for i in range(X.shape[1])])
 507 |     y_norm = _minmax_normalize(y)
 508 | 
 509 |     # x2 is the concentration state c used by ODE RHS templates. During scoring
 510 |     # it is supplied from odeint's dynamic state, not read from candidate code.
 511 |     X_ode = np.column_stack([X_norm, y_norm])
 512 |     return X_ode, y_norm
 513 | 
 514 | 
 515 | def discover_datasets(root: Path = DATA_ROOT) -> list[tuple[str, Path]]:
 516 |     """Recursively find all fit.tsv files and return (dataset_name, path) pairs."""
 517 |     datasets: list[tuple[str, Path]] = []
 518 |     for dirpath, _dirnames, filenames in os.walk(root):
 519 |         if "fit.tsv" in filenames:
 520 |             fit_path = Path(dirpath) / "fit.tsv"
 521 |             rel = fit_path.parent.relative_to(root)
 522 |             name = str(rel).replace(os.sep, "/")
 523 |             datasets.append((name, fit_path))
 524 |     datasets.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
 525 |     return datasets
 526 | 
 527 | 
 528 | def _trajectory_preserving_split(
 529 |     X: np.ndarray,
 530 |     y: np.ndarray,
 531 | ) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
 532 |     """Split by full parameter curves, using late-time holdout for <3 curves."""
 533 |     parameters = np.unique(X[:, 1])
 534 |     rng = np.random.default_rng(RANDOM_STATE)
 535 | 
 536 |     if len(parameters) >= 3:
 537 |         shuffled = parameters[rng.permutation(len(parameters))]
 538 |         n_val = min(max(1, int(round(len(parameters) * TEST_SIZE))), len(parameters) - 1)
 539 |         val_params = set(float(p) for p in shuffled[:n_val])
 540 |         val_mask = np.array([float(p) in val_params for p in X[:, 1]], dtype=bool)
 541 |         train_idx = np.flatnonzero(~val_mask)
 542 |         val_idx = np.flatnonzero(val_mask)
 543 |         return X[train_idx], X[val_idx], y[train_idx], y[val_idx]
 544 | 
 545 |     train_indices: list[int] = []
 546 |     val_indices: list[int] = []
 547 |     for parameter in parameters:
 548 |         curve_idx = np.flatnonzero(X[:, 1] == parameter)
 549 |         ordered_idx = curve_idx[np.argsort(X[curve_idx, 0])]
 550 |         if len(ordered_idx) == 1:
 551 |             train_indices.extend(ordered_idx.tolist())
 552 |             val_indices.extend(ordered_idx.tolist())
 553 |             continue
 554 | 
 555 |         split = int(round(len(ordered_idx) * (1.0 - TEST_SIZE)))
 556 |         split = min(max(split, 1), len(ordered_idx) - 1)
 557 |         train_indices.extend(ordered_idx[:split].tolist())
 558 |         # Include the last training point so validation integration starts from
 559 |         # the known boundary state before predicting the held-out late segment.
 560 |         val_indices.extend(ordered_idx[split - 1 :].tolist())
 561 | 
 562 |     train_idx = np.sort(np.asarray(train_indices, dtype=int))
 563 |     val_idx = np.sort(np.asarray(val_indices, dtype=int))
 564 |     return X[train_idx], X[val_idx], y[train_idx], y[val_idx]
 565 | 
 566 | 
 567 | def load_dataset(data_file: Path) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
 568 |     """Load a single fit.tsv and return deterministic trajectory-preserving split."""
 569 |     X, y = _load_fit_table(data_file)
 570 |     return _trajectory_preserving_split(X, y)
 571 | 
 572 | 
 573 | def load_all_datasets(
 574 |     root: Path = DATA_ROOT,
 575 | ) -> list[tuple[str, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]]:
 576 |     """Load all datasets and return list of (name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val)."""
 577 |     entries = discover_datasets(root)
 578 |     results = []
 579 |     for name, path in entries:
 580 |         try:
 581 |             X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = load_dataset(path)
 582 |             results.append((name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val))
 583 |         except Exception as exc:
 584 |             print(f"Warning: skipping dataset {name}: {exc}")
 585 |     return results
 586 | 
 587 | 
 588 | NUM_WORKERS = int(os.environ.get("SKYDISCOVER_EVAL_WORKERS", min(8, os.cpu_count() or 4)))
 589 | PARSIMONY_WEIGHT = float(os.environ.get("SKYDISCOVER_PARSIMONY_WEIGHT", "0.20"))
 590 | PARSIMONY_COMPLEXITY_SCALE = float(
 591 |     os.environ.get("SKYDISCOVER_PARSIMONY_COMPLEXITY_SCALE", "160")
 592 | )
 593 | 
 594 | 
 595 | def _parsimony_penalty_factor(complexity: float) -> float:
 596 |     """Bounded penalty factor for expression complexity."""
 597 |     if not np.isfinite(complexity) or PARSIMONY_COMPLEXITY_SCALE <= 0:
 598 |         return 1.0
 599 |     normalized = min(max(float(complexity), 0.0) / PARSIMONY_COMPLEXITY_SCALE, 1.0)
 600 |     return max(0.0, 1.0 - PARSIMONY_WEIGHT * normalized)
 601 | 
 602 | 
 603 | def _gaussian_smooth_1d(values: np.ndarray, sigma: float = GAUSSIAN_SMOOTH_SIGMA) -> np.ndarray:
 604 |     values = np.asarray(values, dtype=float).ravel()
 605 |     if values.size < 3 or sigma <= 0.0:
 606 |         return values
 607 |     radius = max(1, int(round(3.0 * sigma)))
 608 |     offsets = np.arange(-radius, radius + 1, dtype=float)
 609 |     kernel = np.exp(-0.5 * (offsets / sigma) ** 2)
 610 |     kernel /= np.sum(kernel)
 611 |     padded = np.pad(values, (radius, radius), mode="edge")
 612 |     return np.convolve(padded, kernel, mode="valid")
 613 | 
 614 | 
 615 | def _curve_smoothed_target(X: np.ndarray, y_true: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
 616 |     """Smooth targets independently for each parameter curve in time order."""
 617 |     X = np.asarray(X, dtype=float)
 618 |     y_true = np.asarray(y_true, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 619 |     if X.shape[0] != y_true.shape[0] or X.shape[1] < 2:
 620 |         return _gaussian_smooth_1d(y_true)
 621 | 
 622 |     smoothed = np.empty_like(y_true, dtype=float)
 623 |     for parameter in np.unique(X[:, 1]):
 624 |         curve_idx = np.flatnonzero(X[:, 1] == parameter)
 625 |         ordered_idx = curve_idx[np.argsort(X[curve_idx, 0])]
 626 |         smoothed_curve = _gaussian_smooth_1d(y_true[ordered_idx])
 627 |         smoothed[ordered_idx] = smoothed_curve
 628 |     return smoothed
 629 | 
 630 | 
 631 | def _nmse_with_gaussian_smoothed_target(
 632 |     y_true: np.ndarray,
 633 |     y_pred: np.ndarray,
 634 |     X: np.ndarray | None = None,
 635 | ) -> float:
 636 |     raw_loss = _base_nmse(y_true, y_pred)
 637 |     smooth_target = _curve_smoothed_target(X, y_true) if X is not None else _gaussian_smooth_1d(y_true)
 638 |     smooth_loss = _base_nmse(smooth_target, y_pred)
 639 |     return float((raw_loss + smooth_loss) / 2.0)
 640 | 
 641 | 
 642 | def _ode_predictions(
 643 |     rhs_fn: Any,
 644 |     X: np.ndarray,
 645 |     y_known: np.ndarray,
 646 |     theta: np.ndarray,
 647 | ) -> np.ndarray:
 648 |     """Integrate dc/dt=f(t, parameter, c) per parameter curve on one split."""
 649 |     from scipy.integrate import odeint
 650 | 
 651 |     X = np.asarray(X, dtype=float)
 652 |     y_known = np.asarray(y_known, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 653 |     theta = np.asarray(theta, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 654 |     predictions = np.full_like(y_known, np.nan, dtype=float)
 655 | 
 656 |     for parameter in np.unique(X[:, 1]):
 657 |         curve_idx = np.flatnonzero(X[:, 1] == parameter)
 658 |         ordered_idx = curve_idx[np.argsort(X[curve_idx, 0])]
 659 |         times = X[ordered_idx, 0]
 660 |         observed = y_known[ordered_idx]
 661 | 
 662 |         if len(ordered_idx) == 1 or float(times[-1] - times[0]) <= 0.0:
 663 |             predictions[ordered_idx] = observed[0]
 664 |             continue
 665 | 
 666 |         def dc_dt(c_state: np.ndarray, time_value: float) -> float:
 667 |             c_value = float(np.asarray(c_state, dtype=float).reshape(-1)[0])
 668 |             value = rhs_fn(float(time_value), float(parameter), c_value, *theta)
 669 |             value = float(np.asarray(value, dtype=float).reshape(-1)[0])
 670 |             if not np.isfinite(value):
 671 |                 raise FloatingPointError("non-finite ODE derivative")
 672 |             return value
 673 | 
 674 |         integrated = odeint(
 675 |             dc_dt,
 676 |             float(observed[0]),
 677 |             times,
 678 |             mxstep=1000,
 679 |         )
 680 |         curve_pred = np.asarray(integrated, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 681 |         if curve_pred.shape != observed.shape or not np.all(np.isfinite(curve_pred)):
 682 |             raise FloatingPointError("non-finite ODE trajectory")
 683 |         predictions[ordered_idx] = curve_pred
 684 | 
 685 |     return predictions
 686 | 
 687 | 
 688 | def evaluate_ode_expression(
 689 |     expression: Any,
 690 |     X_train: np.ndarray,
 691 |     y_train: np.ndarray,
 692 |     X_val: np.ndarray,
 693 |     y_val: np.ndarray,
 694 |     *,
 695 |     constants: Any | None = None,
 696 |     initial_values: Any | None = None,
 697 |     max_nfev: int = 300,
 698 | ) -> dict[str, Any]:
 699 |     """Score a proposed ODE RHS by integrating predicted concentration curves."""
 700 |     import sympy as sp
 701 |     from pysr_harness import equation_session
 702 |     from scipy.optimize import least_squares
 703 | 
 704 |     X_train = np.asarray(X_train, dtype=float)
 705 |     y_train = np.asarray(y_train, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 706 |     X_val = np.asarray(X_val, dtype=float)
 707 |     y_val = np.asarray(y_val, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 708 |     feature_names = [f"x{i}" for i in range(X_train.shape[1])]
 709 |     template = equation_session._as_sympy_expr(expression, feature_names)
 710 |     equation_session._record_scored_template(template)
 711 | 
 712 |     if constants is None:
 713 |         feature_set = set(equation_session.feature_symbols(X_train.shape[1]))
 714 |         constants = sorted(template.free_symbols - feature_set, key=lambda s: s.name)
 715 |     constants = list(constants)
 716 |     equation_session._validate_expression_template(template, constants)
 717 | 
 718 |     if initial_values is None:
 719 |         base_x0 = np.ones(len(constants), dtype=float)
 720 |     else:
 721 |         base_x0 = np.asarray(initial_values, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 722 |         if base_x0.size != len(constants):
 723 |             raise ValueError("initial_values must match the number of constants")
 724 | 
 725 |     feature_syms = equation_session.feature_symbols(X_train.shape[1])
 726 |     rhs_fn = sp.lambdify(feature_syms + constants, template, modules=["numpy"])
 727 | 
 728 |     if not constants:
 729 |         y_pred_train = _ode_predictions(rhs_fn, X_train, y_train, np.asarray([], dtype=float))
 730 |         y_pred_val = _ode_predictions(rhs_fn, X_val, y_val, np.asarray([], dtype=float))
 731 |         nmse_train = _nmse_with_gaussian_smoothed_target(y_train, y_pred_train, X_train)
 732 |         nmse_val = _nmse_with_gaussian_smoothed_target(y_val, y_pred_val, X_val)
 733 |         result = {
 734 |             "equation_template": f"d(c)/dt = {template}",
 735 |             "equation": f"d(c)/dt = {template}",
 736 |             "constants": {},
 737 |             "loss": float(np.mean((y_pred_train - y_train) ** 2)),
 738 |             "complexity": equation_session._complexity(template),
 739 |             "nmse_train": nmse_train,
 740 |             "nmse_val": nmse_val,
 741 |             "combined_score": combined_score_from_nmse(nmse_val),
 742 |         }
 743 |         equation_session._record_scorer_result(result)
 744 |         return result
 745 | 
 746 |     def residual(theta: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
 747 |         try:
 748 |             prediction = _ode_predictions(rhs_fn, X_train, y_train, theta)
 749 |         except Exception:
 750 |             return np.full_like(y_train, 1e12, dtype=float)
 751 |         if prediction.shape != y_train.shape or not np.all(np.isfinite(prediction)):
 752 |             return np.full_like(y_train, 1e12, dtype=float)
 753 |         return prediction - y_train
 754 | 
 755 |     initial_candidates: list[np.ndarray] = []
 756 |     for scale in ODE_MULTISTART_SCALES or (1.0,):
 757 |         candidate = base_x0 * float(scale)
 758 |         if not any(np.array_equal(candidate, existing) for existing in initial_candidates):
 759 |             initial_candidates.append(candidate)
 760 | 
 761 |     import warnings
 762 | 
 763 |     best_fit = None
 764 |     best_loss = float("inf")
 765 |     with warnings.catch_warnings(), np.errstate(over="ignore", divide="ignore", invalid="ignore"):
 766 |         warnings.simplefilter("ignore", RuntimeWarning)
 767 |         for initial_candidate in initial_candidates:
 768 |             fit = least_squares(
 769 |                 residual,
 770 |                 initial_candidate,
 771 |                 loss="soft_l1",
 772 |                 max_nfev=max_nfev,
 773 |             )
 774 |             fit_residual = residual(fit.x)
 775 |             fit_loss = float(np.mean(fit_residual**2))
 776 |             if fit_loss < best_loss:
 777 |                 best_loss = fit_loss
 778 |                 best_fit = fit
 779 | 
 780 |     if best_fit is None:
 781 |         raise RuntimeError("constant fitting did not produce a result")
 782 | 
 783 |     fitted_constants = {
 784 |         str(symbol): float(value) for symbol, value in zip(constants, best_fit.x)
 785 |     }
 786 |     substitutions = dict(zip(constants, best_fit.x))
 787 |     fitted_expr = template.subs(substitutions)
 788 |     y_pred_train = _ode_predictions(rhs_fn, X_train, y_train, best_fit.x)
 789 |     y_pred_val = _ode_predictions(rhs_fn, X_val, y_val, best_fit.x)
 790 |     nmse_train = _nmse_with_gaussian_smoothed_target(y_train, y_pred_train, X_train)
 791 |     nmse_val = _nmse_with_gaussian_smoothed_target(y_val, y_pred_val, X_val)
 792 |     loss = float(np.mean((y_pred_train - y_train) ** 2))
 793 | 
 794 |     result = {
 795 |         "equation_template": f"d(c)/dt = {template}",
 796 |         "equation": f"d(c)/dt = {fitted_expr}",
 797 |         "constants": fitted_constants,
 798 |         "loss": loss,
 799 |         "complexity": equation_session._complexity(template),
 800 |         "nmse_train": nmse_train,
 801 |         "nmse_val": nmse_val,
 802 |         "combined_score": combined_score_from_nmse(nmse_val),
 803 |     }
 804 |     equation_session._record_scorer_result(result)
 805 |     return result
 806 | 
 807 | 
 808 | def _aggregate_per_dataset_results(per_dataset_results: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, Any]:
 809 |     """Aggregate per-dataset metrics without letting failed datasets disappear."""
 810 |     nmse_vals = [r["nmse_val"] for r in per_dataset_results.values()]
 811 |     train_nmse_vals = [
 812 |         float(r["nmse_train"])
 813 |         for r in per_dataset_results.values()
 814 |         if np.isfinite(float(r.get("nmse_train", float("inf"))))
 815 |     ]
 816 |     complexity_vals = []
 817 |     for r in per_dataset_results.values():
 818 |         try:
 819 |             value = float(r.get("complexity", float("nan")))
 820 |         except (TypeError, ValueError):
 821 |             continue
 822 |         if np.isfinite(value):
 823 |             complexity_vals.append(value)
 824 |     aggregate_complexity = float(max(complexity_vals)) if complexity_vals else 0.0
 825 | 
 826 |     violation_errors = sorted(
 827 |         {
 828 |             str(r.get("error"))
 829 |             for r in per_dataset_results.values()
 830 |             if r.get("error") and SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX in str(r.get("error"))
 831 |         }
 832 |     )
 833 |     failed_datasets = sorted(
 834 |         name
 835 |         for name, result in per_dataset_results.items()
 836 |         if result.get("error") or not np.isfinite(float(result.get("nmse_val", float("inf"))))
 837 |     )
 838 |     templates = sorted(
 839 |         {
 840 |             str(r.get("equation_template"))
 841 |             for r in per_dataset_results.values()
 842 |             if r.get("combined_score", 0.0) > 0 and r.get("equation_template")
 843 |         }
 844 |     )
 845 | 
 846 |     eq_template = ""
 847 |     for r in per_dataset_results.values():
 848 |         if r.get("equation"):
 849 |             eq_template = r["equation"]
 850 |             break
 851 | 
 852 |     hard_cases = [
 853 |         {"dataset": name, "nmse_val": float(result["nmse_val"])}
 854 |         for name, result in per_dataset_results.items()
 855 |         if np.isfinite(float(result.get("nmse_val", float("inf"))))
 856 |     ]
 857 |     hard_cases.sort(key=lambda item: item["nmse_val"], reverse=True)
 858 |     hard_cases = hard_cases[:5]
 859 |     hard_case_feedback = "; ".join(
 860 |         f"{case['dataset']} nmse={case['nmse_val']:.4g}" for case in hard_cases
 861 |     )
 862 |     hard_case_tail_nmse = (
 863 |         float(np.mean([case["nmse_val"] for case in hard_cases[:HARD_CASE_TAIL_COUNT]]))
 864 |         if hard_cases and HARD_CASE_TAIL_COUNT > 0
 865 |         else 0.0
 866 |     )
 867 | 
 868 |     violation_error = None
 869 |     if violation_errors:
 870 |         violation_error = violation_errors[0]
 871 |     elif failed_datasets:
 872 |         examples = ", ".join(failed_datasets[:3])
 873 |         violation_error = (
 874 |             "Single-equation violation: candidate failed on "
 875 |             f"{len(failed_datasets)}/{len(per_dataset_results)} datasets. "
 876 |             "Failed datasets are not dropped from the aggregate reward. "
 877 |             f"Examples: {examples}"
 878 |         )
 879 |     elif len(templates) > 1:
 880 |         examples = "; ".join(templates[:3])
 881 |         violation_error = (
 882 |             "Single-equation violation: evaluate_symbolic_candidate() produced "
 883 |             f"{len(templates)} distinct equation templates across datasets. "
 884 |             "The base equation must be identical for all datasets; only fitted "
 885 |             f"constants may vary. Example templates: {examples}"
 886 |         )
 887 | 
 888 |     if violation_error:
 889 |         agg_nmse = float("inf")
 890 |         mean_nmse = float("inf")
 891 |         agg_train_nmse = float("inf")
 892 |         agg_combined = 0.0
 893 |         n_successful = 0
 894 |     else:
 895 |         mean_nmse = float(np.mean(nmse_vals)) if nmse_vals else float("inf")
 896 |         agg_nmse = mean_nmse + HARD_CASE_TAIL_WEIGHT * hard_case_tail_nmse
 897 |         agg_train_nmse = float(np.mean(train_nmse_vals)) if train_nmse_vals else float("inf")
 898 |         raw_combined = (
 899 |             float(1.0 / (1.0 + max(agg_nmse, 0.0))) if np.isfinite(agg_nmse) else 0.0
 900 |         )
 901 |         parsimony_factor = _parsimony_penalty_factor(aggregate_complexity)
 902 |         agg_combined = raw_combined * parsimony_factor
 903 |         n_successful = sum(1 for value in nmse_vals if np.isfinite(value))
 904 | 
 905 |     aggregated = {
 906 |         "equation": eq_template,
 907 |         "nmse_train": agg_train_nmse,
 908 |         "nmse_val": agg_nmse,
 909 |         "mean_nmse_val": mean_nmse,
 910 |         "combined_score": agg_combined,
 911 |         "fit_score": raw_combined if not violation_error else 0.0,
 912 |         "complexity": aggregate_complexity,
 913 |         "parsimony_weight": PARSIMONY_WEIGHT,
 914 |         "parsimony_penalty_factor": (
 915 |             _parsimony_penalty_factor(aggregate_complexity) if not violation_error else 1.0
 916 |         ),
 917 |         "n_datasets": len(per_dataset_results),
 918 |         "n_successful": n_successful,
 919 |         "eval_workers": NUM_WORKERS,
 920 |         "hard_cases": hard_cases,
 921 |         "hard_case_feedback": hard_case_feedback,
 922 |         "hard_case_tail_nmse": hard_case_tail_nmse,
 923 |         "hard_case_tail_weight": HARD_CASE_TAIL_WEIGHT,
 924 |         "per_dataset": per_dataset_results,
 925 |     }
 926 |     if violation_error:
 927 |         aggregated["error"] = violation_error
 928 |     return aggregated
 929 | 
 930 | 
 931 | def evaluate_candidate_with_timeout(program_path: str, timeout_seconds: int = 3000) -> dict:
 932 |     """Evaluate a candidate program across ALL datasets in a subprocess.
 933 | 
 934 |     The subprocess loads every dataset, calls the candidate's
 935 |     evaluate_symbolic_candidate() once per dataset (fitting independent
 936 |     constants each time) using parallel threads, and returns aggregated
 937 |     metrics.
 938 |     """
 939 |     validate_candidate_source(program_path)
 940 |     harness_root = str(BENCHMARK_ROOT)
 941 |     evaluator_path = str(Path(__file__).resolve())
 942 |     with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".py", delete=False) as temp_file:
 943 |         script = f"""
 944 | import importlib.util
 945 | import os
 946 | import pickle
 947 | import sys
 948 | import traceback
 949 | from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed
 950 | 
 951 | os.environ.setdefault("OMP_NUM_THREADS", "1")
 952 | os.environ.setdefault("MKL_NUM_THREADS", "1")
 953 | os.environ.setdefault("OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS", "1")
 954 | 
 955 | import numpy as np
 956 | 
 957 | sys.path.insert(0, {harness_root!r})
 958 | 
 959 | program_path = {program_path!r}
 960 | evaluator_path = {evaluator_path!r}
 961 | results_path = {temp_file.name + ".results"!r}
 962 | NUM_WORKERS = int(os.environ.get("SKYDISCOVER_EVAL_WORKERS", min(8, os.cpu_count() or 4)))
 963 | 
 964 | def _write_payload_and_exit(payload):
 965 |     with open(results_path, "wb") as f:
 966 |         pickle.dump(payload, f)
 967 |     sys.stdout.flush()
 968 |     sys.stderr.flush()
 969 |     # PySR/PythonCall can leave Julia runtime threads/finalizers alive at
 970 |     # interpreter shutdown. The parent only needs the pickle payload.
 971 |     os._exit(0)
 972 | 
 973 | try:
 974 |     evaluator_spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("benchmark_evaluator", evaluator_path)
 975 |     evaluator = importlib.util.module_from_spec(evaluator_spec)
 976 |     evaluator_spec.loader.exec_module(evaluator)
 977 |     evaluator.validate_candidate_source(program_path)
 978 | 
 979 |     import pysr_harness.equation_session as equation_session
 980 |     equation_session.evaluate_expression = evaluator.evaluate_ode_expression
 981 |     equation_session.nmse = evaluator._nmse_with_gaussian_smoothed_target
 982 |     spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("program", program_path)
 983 |     program = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
 984 |     spec.loader.exec_module(program)
 985 |     from pysr_harness.equation_session import (
 986 |         single_equation_evaluation,
 987 |         validate_single_equation_result,
 988 |     )
 989 | 
 990 |     fn = getattr(program, "evaluate_symbolic_candidate", None) or getattr(
 991 |         program, "run_discovery", None
 992 |     )
 993 |     if fn is None:
 994 |         raise AttributeError("Program must define evaluate_symbolic_candidate() or run_discovery()")
 995 | 
 996 |     datasets = evaluator.load_all_datasets()
 997 | 
 998 |     def _eval_one(args):
 999 |         ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = args
1000 |         try:
1001 |             with single_equation_evaluation():
1002 |                 result = fn(X_train, y_train, X_val, y_val)
1003 |                 validate_single_equation_result(result)
1004 |             ds_nmse = float(result.get("nmse_val", float("inf")))
1005 |             ds_train_nmse = float(result.get("nmse_train", float("inf")))
1006 |             ds_combined = float(result.get("combined_score", 0.0))
1007 |             return ds_name, {{
1008 |                 "nmse_train": ds_train_nmse,
1009 |                 "nmse_val": ds_nmse,
1010 |                 "combined_score": ds_combined,
1011 |                 "complexity": float(result.get("complexity", 0.0)),
1012 |                 "equation": str(result.get("equation", "")),
1013 |                 "equation_template": str(result.get("equation_template", "")),
1014 |                 "constants": result.get("constants", {{}}),
1015 |             }}
1016 |         except Exception as exc:
1017 |             return ds_name, {{
1018 |                 "nmse_train": float("inf"),
1019 |                 "nmse_val": float("inf"),
1020 |                 "combined_score": 0.0,
1021 |                 "error": str(exc),
1022 |             }}
1023 | 
1024 |     per_dataset_results = {{}}
1025 |     with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=NUM_WORKERS) as executor:
1026 |         futures = [executor.submit(_eval_one, dataset) for dataset in datasets]
1027 |         for future in as_completed(futures):
1028 |             ds_name, ds_result = future.result()
1029 |             per_dataset_results[ds_name] = ds_result
1030 | 
1031 |     aggregated = evaluator._aggregate_per_dataset_results(per_dataset_results)
1032 | 
1033 |     _write_payload_and_exit({{"result": aggregated}})
1034 | except Exception as exc:
1035 |     traceback.print_exc()
1036 |     _write_payload_and_exit({{"error": str(exc)}})
1037 | """
1038 |         temp_file.write(script.encode())
1039 |         temp_path = temp_file.name
1040 | 
1041 |     results_path = f"{temp_path}.results"
1042 |     try:
1043 |         process = subprocess.Popen(
1044 |             [sys.executable, temp_path],
1045 |             stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
1046 |             stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
1047 |         )
1048 |         stdout, stderr = process.communicate(timeout=timeout_seconds)
1049 |         if stdout:
1050 |             print(stdout.decode())
1051 |         if stderr:
1052 |             print(stderr.decode())
1053 |         if process.returncode != 0:
1054 |             raise RuntimeError(f"Subprocess exited with code {process.returncode}")
1055 |         if not os.path.exists(results_path):
1056 |             raise RuntimeError("Results file not found")
1057 |         with open(results_path, "rb") as f:
1058 |             payload = pickle.load(f)
1059 |         if "error" in payload:
1060 |             raise RuntimeError(payload["error"])
1061 |         return payload["result"]
1062 |     except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
1063 |         process.kill()
1064 |         process.wait()
1065 |         raise TimeoutError(f"Program execution timed out after {timeout_seconds}s")
1066 |     finally:
1067 |         for path in (temp_path, results_path):
1068 |             if os.path.exists(path):
1069 |                 os.unlink(path)
1070 | 
1071 | 
1072 | def _metrics_from_result(result: dict, eval_time: float) -> dict:
1073 |     """Normalize aggregated harness output into SkyDiscover evaluator metrics."""
1074 |     nmse_val = float(result.get("nmse_val", float("inf")))
1075 |     combined = float(result.get("combined_score", combined_score_from_nmse(nmse_val)))
1076 |     if not np.isfinite(combined):
1077 |         combined = 0.0
1078 | 
1079 |     return {
1080 |         "equation": str(result.get("equation", "")),
1081 |         "loss": nmse_val,
1082 |         "complexity": float(result.get("complexity", 0.0)),
1083 |         "nmse_train": float(result.get("nmse_train", float("inf"))),
1084 |         "nmse_val": nmse_val,
1085 |         "mean_nmse_val": float(result.get("mean_nmse_val", nmse_val)),
1086 |         "combined_score": combined,
1087 |         "fit_score": float(result.get("fit_score", combined)),
1088 |         "parsimony_weight": float(result.get("parsimony_weight", PARSIMONY_WEIGHT)),
1089 |         "parsimony_penalty_factor": float(result.get("parsimony_penalty_factor", 1.0)),
1090 |         "eval_time": float(eval_time),
1091 |         "n_datasets": result.get("n_datasets", 0),
1092 |         "n_successful": result.get("n_successful", 0),
1093 |         "eval_workers": result.get("eval_workers", NUM_WORKERS),
1094 |         "hard_cases": result.get("hard_cases", []),
1095 |         "hard_case_feedback": str(result.get("hard_case_feedback", "")),
1096 |         "hard_case_tail_nmse": float(result.get("hard_case_tail_nmse", 0.0)),
1097 |         "hard_case_tail_weight": float(result.get("hard_case_tail_weight", HARD_CASE_TAIL_WEIGHT)),
1098 |         "per_dataset": result.get("per_dataset", {}),
1099 |         "error": str(result.get("error", "")),
1100 |     }
1101 | 
1102 | 
1103 | def evaluate(program_path: str) -> dict:
1104 |     """Evaluate one evolved symbolic-equation candidate across all datasets."""
1105 |     try:
1106 |         start = time.time()
1107 |         result = evaluate_candidate_with_timeout(program_path, timeout_seconds=3000)
1108 |         metrics = _metrics_from_result(result, time.time() - start)
1109 |         print(
1110 |             "Evaluation: "
1111 |             f"nmse_val={metrics['nmse_val']:.6f}, "
1112 |             f"mean_nmse_val={metrics['mean_nmse_val']:.6f}, "
1113 |             f"combined_score={metrics['combined_score']:.6f}, "
1114 |             f"n_datasets={metrics['n_datasets']}, "
1115 |             f"n_successful={metrics['n_successful']}, "
1116 |             f"time={metrics['eval_time']:.2f}s"
1117 |         )
1118 |         print(f"Equation template: {metrics['equation']}")
1119 |         if metrics.get("hard_case_feedback"):
1120 |             print(f"Hard-case feedback: {metrics['hard_case_feedback']}")
1121 |         if metrics.get("error"):
1122 |             print(f"Evaluator feedback: {metrics['error']}")
1123 |         return metrics
1124 |     except Exception as exc:
1125 |         print(f"Evaluation failed: {exc}")
1126 |         traceback.print_exc()
1127 |         return {
1128 |             "equation": "",
1129 |             "loss": float("inf"),
1130 |             "complexity": 0.0,
1131 |             "nmse_train": float("inf"),
1132 |             "nmse_val": float("inf"),
1133 |             "mean_nmse_val": float("inf"),
1134 |             "combined_score": 0.0,
1135 |             "eval_time": 0.0,
1136 |             "n_datasets": 0,
1137 |             "n_successful": 0,
1138 |             "hard_cases": [],
1139 |             "hard_case_feedback": "",
1140 |             "hard_case_tail_nmse": 0.0,
1141 |             "hard_case_tail_weight": HARD_CASE_TAIL_WEIGHT,
1142 |             "per_dataset": {},
1143 |             "error": str(exc),
1144 |         }
1145 | 
1146 | 
1147 | def _eval_stage1_worker(args):
1148 |     """Module-level worker for stage 1 parallel evaluation."""
1149 |     name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val, program_path = args
1150 |     harness_root = str(BENCHMARK_ROOT)
1151 |     if harness_root not in sys.path:
1152 |         sys.path.insert(0, harness_root)
1153 |     validate_candidate_source(program_path)
1154 |     import pysr_harness.equation_session as equation_session
1155 |     equation_session.evaluate_expression = evaluate_ode_expression
1156 |     equation_session.nmse = _nmse_with_gaussian_smoothed_target
1157 |     spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("program", program_path)
1158 |     program = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
1159 |     spec.loader.exec_module(program)
1160 |     from pysr_harness.equation_session import (
1161 |         single_equation_evaluation,
1162 |         validate_single_equation_result,
1163 |     )
1164 | 
1165 |     fn = getattr(program, "evaluate_symbolic_candidate", None) or getattr(
1166 |         program, "run_discovery", None
1167 |     )
1168 |     if fn is None:
1169 |         return 0.0, "missing evaluate_symbolic_candidate()", ""
1170 |     try:
1171 |         with single_equation_evaluation():
1172 |             result = fn(X_train, y_train, X_val, y_val)
1173 |             validate_single_equation_result(result)
1174 |         return float(result.get("combined_score", 0.0)), None, str(
1175 |             result.get("equation_template", "")
1176 |         )
1177 |     except Exception as exc:
1178 |         return 0.0, str(exc), ""
1179 | 
1180 | 
1181 | def evaluate_stage1(program_path: str) -> dict:
1182 |     """Fast cascade stage: evaluate on a small subset of datasets with reduced data."""
1183 |     try:
1184 |         harness_root = str(BENCHMARK_ROOT)
1185 |         if harness_root not in sys.path:
1186 |             sys.path.insert(0, harness_root)
1187 | 
1188 |         all_datasets = load_all_datasets()
1189 |         rng = np.random.default_rng(RANDOM_STATE)
1190 |         if len(all_datasets) > 8:
1191 |             indices = rng.choice(len(all_datasets), size=8, replace=False)
1192 |             subset = [all_datasets[i] for i in sorted(indices)]
1193 |         else:
1194 |             subset = all_datasets
1195 | 
1196 |         work_items = [
1197 |             (name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val, program_path)
1198 |             for name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in subset
1199 |         ]
1200 | 
1201 |         with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=NUM_WORKERS) as executor:
1202 |             futures = [executor.submit(_eval_stage1_worker, item) for item in work_items]
1203 |             worker_results = [future.result() for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures)]
1204 | 
1205 |         combined_scores = [score for score, _error, _template in worker_results]
1206 |         successful_scores = [score for score in combined_scores if score > 0]
1207 |         errors = sorted({error for _score, error, _template in worker_results if error})
1208 |         violation_errors = sorted(
1209 |             {
1210 |                 error
1211 |                 for _score, error, _template in worker_results
1212 |                 if error and SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX in error
1213 |             }
1214 |         )
1215 |         if violation_errors:
1216 |             return {
1217 |                 "combined_score": 0.0,
1218 |                 "n_stage1_datasets": len(subset),
1219 |                 "n_stage1_successful": len(successful_scores),
1220 |                 "eval_workers": NUM_WORKERS,
1221 |                 "error": violation_errors[0],
1222 |             }
1223 |         if errors:
1224 |             return {
1225 |                 "combined_score": 0.0,
1226 |                 "n_stage1_datasets": len(subset),
1227 |                 "n_stage1_successful": len(successful_scores),
1228 |                 "eval_workers": NUM_WORKERS,
1229 |                 "error": (
1230 |                     "Single-equation violation: stage 1 candidate failed on "
1231 |                     f"{len(errors)} dataset evaluations. Example: {errors[0]}"
1232 |                 ),
1233 |             }
1234 |         templates = sorted(
1235 |             {
1236 |                 template
1237 |                 for score, _error, template in worker_results
1238 |                 if score > 0 and template
1239 |             }
1240 |         )
1241 |         if len(templates) > 1:
1242 |             examples = "; ".join(templates[:3])
1243 |             return {
1244 |                 "combined_score": 0.0,
1245 |                 "n_stage1_datasets": len(subset),
1246 |                 "n_stage1_successful": len(successful_scores),
1247 |                 "eval_workers": NUM_WORKERS,
1248 |                 "error": (
1249 |                     "Single-equation violation: stage 1 produced "
1250 |                     f"{len(templates)} distinct equation templates across datasets. "
1251 |                     "The base equation must be identical for all datasets. "
1252 |                     f"Example templates: {examples}"
1253 |                 ),
1254 |             }
1255 |         agg_combined = float(np.mean(combined_scores)) if combined_scores else 0.0
1256 |         return {
1257 |             "combined_score": agg_combined,
1258 |             "n_stage1_datasets": len(subset),
1259 |             "n_stage1_successful": len(successful_scores),
1260 |             "eval_workers": NUM_WORKERS,
1261 |             "stage1_full_selected_datasets": 1.0,
1262 |         }
1263 |     except Exception as exc:
1264 |         print(f"Stage 1 evaluation failed: {exc}")
1265 |         return {"combined_score": 0.0, "error": str(exc)}
1266 | 
1267 | 
1268 | def evaluate_stage2(program_path: str) -> dict:
1269 |     """Full evaluation for cascade stage 2."""
1270 |     return evaluate(program_path)

import csv, os, numpy as np
from pathlib import Path
root = Path('/home/mrsar/protein-ag/past-published-data/disease-relevant non-inhibited')
datasets = []
for dirpath, _, filenames in os.walk(root):
    if 'fit.tsv' in filenames:
        datasets.append(Path(dirpath) / 'fit.tsv')
datasets.sort()

# Check all datasets for number of curves and data shape
for p in datasets:
    rel = p.parent.relative_to(root)
    with open(p) as f:
        reader = csv.reader(f, delimiter='\t')
        header = next(reader)
        rows = [[float(v) if v.strip() and v.strip().lower() != 'nan' else float('nan') for v in r] for r in reader if r]
    data = np.array(rows)
    times = data[:,0]
    responses = data[:,1:]
    ncurves = responses.shape[1]
    # Check if data is already sigmoidal (starts low, ends high)
    first_vals = [responses[0,i] for i in range(ncurves) if not np.isnan(responses[0,i])]
    last_vals = [responses[-1,i] for i in range(ncurves) if not np.isnan(responses[-1,i])]
    avg_first = np.mean(first_vals) if first_vals else float('nan')
    avg_last = np.mean(last_vals) if last_vals else float('nan')
    print(f'{rel}: {ncurves} curves, t=[{times.min():.2f},{times.max():.2f}], first={avg_first:.3f}, last={avg_last:.3f}')

import numpy as np
from scipy.integrate import odeint

t = np.linspace(0, 1, 100)

# Test 1: Richards/generalized logistic
# dc/dt = c0 * x2^c1 * (1 - x2)
# Hill-like: c1<1 -> fast early, c1>1 -> slow early (lag phase)
def richards(c, t, k, n):
    c = max(c, 1e-10)
    return k * (c**n) * (1 - c)

for k, n in [(20, 0.5), (20, 1.0), (20, 2.0), (5, 0.3)]:
    sol = odeint(richards, 0.001, t, args=(k, n))
    print(f'Richards k={k},n={n}: [{sol.min():.3f},{sol.max():.3f}], t50={np.argmax(sol[:,0]>0.5)}')

print()
# Test 2: dc/dt = c0*(1-x2) + c1*x2^c2*(1-x2)
# Finke-Watzky with generalized autocatalysis
def fw_gen(c, t, k1, k2, n):
    c = max(c, 0.0)
    return k1*(1-c) + k2*(c**n)*(1-c)

for k1, k2, n in [(0.01, 20, 1.0), (0.01, 20, 0.5), (0.01, 20, 2.0), (0.1, 15, 1.5)]:
    sol = odeint(fw_gen, 0.0, t, args=(k1, k2, n))
    print(f'FW-gen k1={k1},k2={k2},n={n}: [{sol.min():.3f},{sol.max():.3f}], t50={np.argmax(sol[:,0]>0.5)}')
"

import numpy as np
from scipy.integrate import odeint

t = np.linspace(0, 1, 100)

# Option C: additive x1 modulation - more stable than multiplicative
# dc/dt = c0*(1-x2) + (c1 + c2*x1)*x2*(1-x2)
# At x1=0: dc/dt = c0*(1-x2) + c1*x2*(1-x2)
# At x1=1: dc/dt = c0*(1-x2) + (c1+c2)*x2*(1-x2)
# Rate is always positive if c1>0, c2>-c1 (robust)

def optC(c, t, c0, c1, c2, x1):
    return c0*(1-c) + (c1 + c2*x1)*c*(1-c)

print('Option C (additive x1):')
for x1 in [0.0, 0.5, 1.0]:
    sol = odeint(optC, 0.0, t, args=(0.01, 15.0, 10.0, x1))
    print(f'  x1={x1}: [{sol.min():.3f},{sol.max():.3f}], t50={np.argmax(sol[:,0]>0.5)}')

print()
# Option A: free plateau c3
# dc/dt = c0*(c3-x2) + c1*(1+c2*x1)*x2*(c3-x2)
def optA(c, t, c0, c1, c2, c3, x1):
    return c0*(c3-c) + c1*(1+c2*x1)*c*(c3-c)

print('Option A (free plateau):')
for x1, c3 in [(0.0, 1.0), (0.5, 1.0), (1.0, 1.0), (0.5, 0.95)]:
    sol = odeint(optA, 0.0, t, args=(0.01, 20.0, 2.0, c3, x1))
    print(f'  x1={x1},c3={c3}: [{sol.min():.3f},{sol.max():.3f}], t50={np.argmax(sol[:,0]>0.5)}')

print()
# Test the current model on a hard case: seeded data starting at 0.036
def current(c, t, c0, c1, c2, x1):
    return c0*(1-c) + c1*(1+c2*x1)*c*(1-c)

print('Current model seeded (c0_init=0.036):')
for x1 in [0.0, 0.5, 1.0]:
    sol = odeint(current, 0.036, t, args=(0.01, 20.0, 2.0, x1))
    print(f'  x1={x1}: [{sol.min():.3f},{sol.max():.3f}]')
"

import sympy as sp

# Count complexity of each expression
# Current: c0*(1-x2) + c1*(1+c2*x1)*x2*(1-x2)
# New additive: c0*(1-x2) + (c1+c2*x1)*x2*(1-x2)

x0, x1, x2 = sp.symbols('x0 x1 x2')
c0, c1, c2, c3 = sp.symbols('c0 c1 c2 c3')

expr_current = c0*(1-x2) + c1*(1+c2*x1)*x2*(1-x2)
expr_additive = c0*(1-x2) + (c1+c2*x1)*x2*(1-x2)
expr_plateau = c0*(c3-x2) + c1*(1+c2*x1)*x2*(c3-x2)

def count_ops(expr):
    return sum(1 for _ in sp.preorder_traversal(expr))

print('Current complexity:', count_ops(expr_current), '| expr:', expr_current)
print('Additive complexity:', count_ops(expr_additive), '| expr:', expr_additive)
print('Plateau complexity:', count_ops(expr_plateau), '| expr:', expr_plateau)

# Are they equivalent?
diff = sp.expand(expr_current - expr_additive)
print('Current - Additive =', diff)  # Should show they differ

import pandas as pd
import os
base = '/home/mrsar/protein-ag/past-published-data/disease-relevant non-inhibited/'
datasets = []
for root, dirs, files in os.walk(base):
    for f in files:
        if f == 'fit.tsv':
            datasets.append(os.path.join(root, f))

# Look at a multi-column dataset
for d in datasets:
    df = pd.read_csv(d, sep='\t')
    if df.shape[1] > 3:
        print(f'\n{d.replace(base,\"\")}')
        print(f'  shape: {df.shape}, cols: {list(df.columns)}')
        print(df.head(3).to_string())
        break

# Also show a single-column example
for d in datasets[:3]:
    df = pd.read_csv(d, sep='\t')
    print(f'\n{d.replace(base,\"\")}')
    print(f'  shape: {df.shape}')
    print(df.head(5).to_string())
    break
