 258 | def _allowed_feature_symbols_data_ids(fn: ast.FunctionDef) -> set[int]:
 259 |     allowed: set[int] = set()
 260 |     for node in ast.walk(fn):
 261 |         if not (
 262 |             isinstance(node, ast.Call)
 263 |             and isinstance(node.func, ast.Name)
 264 |             and node.func.id == "feature_symbols"
 265 |             and len(node.args) == 1
 266 |         ):
 267 |             continue
 268 |         x_train_id = _x_train_shape_feature_arg_id(node.args[0])
 269 |         if x_train_id is not None:
 270 |             allowed.add(x_train_id)
 271 |     return allowed

 274 | def _validate_no_dataset_data_leak(fn: ast.FunctionDef, allowed_node_ids: set[int]) -> None:
 275 |     for node in ast.walk(fn):
 276 |         if (
 277 |             isinstance(node, ast.Name)
 278 |             and node.id in {"X_train", "y_train", "X_val", "y_val"}
 279 |             and id(node) not in allowed_node_ids
 280 |         ):
 281 |             raise ValueError(
 282 |                 f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: dataset array '{node.id}' may only "
 283 |                 "appear in feature_symbols(X_train.shape[1]) or as the canonical "
 284 |                 "evaluate_expression(expression, X_train, y_train, X_val, y_val, ...) "
 285 |                 "arguments. Do not use dataset values to choose expressions, mutate "
 286 |                 "splits, or tune constants/initial values."
 287 |             )

 290 | def validate_candidate_source(program_path: str) -> None:
 291 |     """Reject reward-hacking program structure before importing candidate code."""
 292 |     source = Path(program_path).read_text()
 293 |     tree = ast.parse(source, filename=program_path)
 294 |     postponed_annotations = _has_postponed_annotations(tree)
 295 | 
 296 |     for node in tree.body:
 297 |         if _is_module_docstring(node) or _is_main_guard(node):
 298 |             continue
 299 |         if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef):
 300 |             _validate_top_level_function(node, postponed_annotations=postponed_annotations)
 301 |             continue
 302 |         if isinstance(node, ast.Import):
 303 |             for alias in node.names:
 304 |                 _validate_plain_import(alias)
 305 |         elif isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom):
 306 |             module = node.module or ""
 307 |             if module not in _ALLOWED_FROM_IMPORTS:
 308 |                 raise ValueError(
 309 |                     f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: from-import '{module}' is not allowed."
 310 |                 )
 311 |             if module == "pysr_harness.equation_session":
 312 |                 aliased = [alias.name for alias in node.names if alias.asname]
 313 |                 if aliased:
 314 |                     raise ValueError(
 315 |                         f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: harness imports may not "
 316 |                         f"use aliases: {sorted(aliased)}."
 317 |                     )
 318 |                 imported = {alias.name for alias in node.names}
 319 |                 extra = imported - _ALLOWED_HARNESS_IMPORTS
 320 |                 if extra:
 321 |                     raise ValueError(
 322 |                         f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: unsupported harness imports "
 323 |                         f"{sorted(extra)}. Use only feature_symbols, constant_symbols, "
 324 |                         "and evaluate_expression."
 325 |                     )
 326 |         else:
 327 |             raise ValueError(
 328 |                 f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: top-level executable code is not "
 329 |                 "allowed in candidate programs. Build and score the equation only "
 330 |                 "inside evaluate_symbolic_candidate()."
 331 |             )
 332 | 
 333 |     eval_fns = [
 334 |         node
 335 |         for node in tree.body
 336 |         if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef) and node.name == "evaluate_symbolic_candidate"
 337 |     ]
 338 |     if len(eval_fns) != 1:
 339 |         raise ValueError(
 340 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: program must define exactly one "
 341 |             "evaluate_symbolic_candidate() function."
 342 |         )
 343 |     fn = eval_fns[0]
 344 | 
 345 |     returns = [node for node in ast.walk(fn) if isinstance(node, ast.Return)]
 346 |     if len(returns) != 1:
 347 |         raise ValueError(
 348 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: evaluate_symbolic_candidate() must have "
 349 |             "exactly one return statement."
 350 |         )
 351 |     return_value = returns[0].value
 352 |     if not (
 353 |         isinstance(return_value, ast.Call)
 354 |         and isinstance(return_value.func, ast.Name)
 355 |         and return_value.func.id == "evaluate_expression"
 356 |     ):
 357 |         raise ValueError(
 358 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: evaluate_symbolic_candidate() must directly "
 359 |             "return evaluate_expression(...), with no wrapper or post-processing."
 360 |         )
 361 |     allowed_data_arg_ids = _validate_evaluate_expression_data_args(return_value)
 362 |     allowed_data_arg_ids.update(_allowed_feature_symbols_data_ids(fn))
 363 |     _validate_no_dataset_data_leak(fn, allowed_data_arg_ids)
 364 | 
 365 |     evaluate_calls = 0
 366 |     for node in ast.walk(fn):
 367 |         if isinstance(node, _DISALLOWED_CONTROL_NODES):
 368 |             raise ValueError(
 369 |                 f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: control flow, nested definitions, "
 370 |                 "comprehensions, and conditionals are not allowed inside "
 371 |                 "evaluate_symbolic_candidate(). Build one straight-line equation."
 372 |             )
 373 |         if isinstance(node, (ast.Import, ast.ImportFrom)):
 374 |             raise ValueError(
 375 |                 f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: imports are not allowed inside "
 376 |                 "evaluate_symbolic_candidate()."
 377 |             )
 378 |         if isinstance(node, (ast.Assign, ast.AnnAssign, ast.AugAssign)):
 379 |             targets = node.targets if isinstance(node, ast.Assign) else [node.target]
 380 |             assigned = set().union(*(_assigned_names(target) for target in targets))
 381 |             protected = assigned & _PROTECTED_NAMES
 382 |             if protected:
 383 |                 raise ValueError(
 384 |                     f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: cannot reassign protected scorer "
 385 |                     f"or harness names {sorted(protected)}."
 386 |                 )
 387 |         if isinstance(node, ast.Call):
 388 |             name = _call_name(node.func)
 389 |             if name == "evaluate_expression":
 390 |                 evaluate_calls += 1
 391 |             if name not in _ALLOWED_EVALUATE_CALLS:
 392 |                 raise ValueError(
 393 |                     f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: call '{name or type(node.func).__name__}' "
 394 |                     "is not allowed inside evaluate_symbolic_candidate(). Allowed calls "
 395 |                     "are the symbol helpers, evaluate_expression, and basic sp.* math."
 396 |                 )
 397 | 
 398 |     if evaluate_calls != 1:
 399 |         raise ValueError(
 400 |             f"{SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX}: evaluate_symbolic_candidate() must call "
 401 |             f"evaluate_expression() exactly once; found {evaluate_calls} calls."
 402 |         )
 403 | 
 404 | 
 405 | def _parse_parameter_from_column(column_name: str) -> float | None:
 406 |     """Extract a numeric parameter value from a fit.tsv column header.
 407 | 
 408 |     Handles diverse header formats found across disease-relevant datasets:
 409 |       - "... : 10uM"  or "10 uM"  → 10.0
 410 |       - "35uM" (bare, as in Abeta) → 35.0
 411 |       - "... : 3.95mM"             → 3950.0  (converted to uM)
 412 |       - "... : 0.3 mg/mL 22uM"    → 22.0    (prefer uM when both present)
 413 |       - "... : pH 2.7"             → 2.7
 414 |       - "... : 5% seeds"           → 5.0
 415 |       - "20 ng/ul"                 → 20.0
 416 |       - Non-numeric labels (WT, C19S, acid 10min, ...) → None
 417 |     """
 418 |     text = column_name.strip()
 419 | 
 420 |     # Prefer an explicit uM value
 421 |     m = re.search(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*uM", text, re.IGNORECASE)
 422 |     if m:
 423 |         return float(m.group(1))
 424 | 
 425 |     # mM → convert to uM
 426 |     m = re.search(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*mM", text, re.IGNORECASE)
 427 |     if m:
 428 |         return float(m.group(1)) * 1000.0
 429 | 
 430 |     # mg/mL (take the number)
 431 |     m = re.search(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*mg/mL", text, re.IGNORECASE)
 432 |     if m:
 433 |         return float(m.group(1))
 434 | 
 435 |     # ng/ul
 436 |     m = re.search(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*ng/ul", text, re.IGNORECASE)
 437 |     if m:
 438 |         return float(m.group(1))
 439 | 
 440 |     # pH value
 441 |     m = re.search(r"pH\s+(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)", text, re.IGNORECASE)
 442 |     if m:
 443 |         return float(m.group(1))
 444 | 
 445 |     # Percentage (e.g. "5% seeds")
 446 |     m = re.search(r"(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*%", text)
 447 |     if m:
 448 |         return float(m.group(1))
 449 | 
 450 |     # Last resort: try to find any trailing number after colon/space
 451 |     m = re.search(r":\s*(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)\s*$", text)
 452 |     if m:
 453 |         return float(m.group(1))
 454 | 
 455 |     return None
 456 | 
 457 | 
 458 | def _minmax_normalize(values: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
 459 |     """Normalize one dataset column to 0..1, preserving constants as zeros."""
 460 |     values = np.asarray(values, dtype=float)
 461 |     v_min = float(np.min(values))
 462 |     v_max = float(np.max(values))
 463 |     scale = v_max - v_min
 464 |     if scale <= 0.0:
 465 |         return np.zeros_like(values, dtype=float)
 466 |     return (values - v_min) / scale
 467 | 
 468 | 
 469 | def _load_fit_table(data_file: Path) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
 470 |     """Load a wide fit.tsv and return normalized X=[time, parameter, c], y.
 471 | 
 472 |     The first column is always the time/measurement coordinate. Remaining
 473 |     columns are response curves. The varying parameter (concentration, pH,
 474 |     etc.) is extracted from the header; if extraction fails, sequential
 475 |     indices 1, 2, 3, ... are used. Each dataset is then min-max normalized:
 476 |     time and parameter independently to 0..1, and the response globally across
 477 |     the whole fit.tsv to 0..1. Files already rescaled to 0..1 keep that scale.
 478 |     """
 479 |     with data_file.open(newline="") as f:
 480 |         reader = csv.reader(f, delimiter="\t")
 481 |         header = next(reader)
 482 | 
 483 |         response_headers = header[1:]
 484 |         parsed_params = [_parse_parameter_from_column(h) for h in response_headers]
 485 |         if all(p is not None for p in parsed_params):
 486 |             parameters = [float(p) for p in parsed_params]
 487 |         else:
 488 |             parameters = [float(i + 1) for i in range(len(response_headers))]
 489 | 
 490 |         features: list[list[float]] = []
 491 |         targets: list[float] = []
 492 |         for row in reader:
 493 |             if not row:
 494 |                 continue
 495 |             measurement_x = float(row[0])
 496 |             for param, value in zip(parameters, row[1:]):
 497 |                 if value == "" or value.strip().lower() == "nan":
 498 |                     continue
 499 |                 features.append([measurement_x, param])
 500 |                 targets.append(float(value))
 501 | 
 502 |     if not features:
 503 |         raise ValueError(f"No regression samples loaded from {data_file}")
 504 | 
 505 |     X = np.asarray(features, dtype=float)
 506 |     y = np.asarray(targets, dtype=float)
 507 |     X_norm = np.column_stack([_minmax_normalize(X[:, i]) for i in range(X.shape[1])])
 508 |     y_norm = _minmax_normalize(y)
 509 | 
 510 |     # x2 is the concentration state c used by ODE RHS templates. During scoring
 511 |     # it is supplied from odeint's dynamic state, not read from candidate code.
 512 |     X_ode = np.column_stack([X_norm, y_norm])
 513 |     return X_ode, y_norm
 514 | 
 515 | 
 516 | def discover_datasets(root: Path = DATA_ROOT) -> list[tuple[str, Path]]:
 517 |     """Recursively find all fit.tsv files and return (dataset_name, path) pairs."""
 518 |     datasets: list[tuple[str, Path]] = []
 519 |     for dirpath, _dirnames, filenames in os.walk(root):
 520 |         if "fit.tsv" in filenames:
 521 |             fit_path = Path(dirpath) / "fit.tsv"
 522 |             rel = fit_path.parent.relative_to(root)
 523 |             name = str(rel).replace(os.sep, "/")
 524 |             datasets.append((name, fit_path))
 525 |     datasets.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])
 526 |     return datasets
 527 | 
 528 | 
 529 | def _trajectory_preserving_split(
 530 |     X: np.ndarray,
 531 |     y: np.ndarray,
 532 | ) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
 533 |     """Split by full parameter curves, using late-time holdout for <3 curves."""
 534 |     parameters = np.unique(X[:, 1])
 535 |     rng = np.random.default_rng(RANDOM_STATE)
 536 | 
 537 |     if len(parameters) >= 3:
 538 |         shuffled = parameters[rng.permutation(len(parameters))]
 539 |         n_val = min(max(1, int(round(len(parameters) * TEST_SIZE))), len(parameters) - 1)
 540 |         val_params = set(float(p) for p in shuffled[:n_val])
 541 |         val_mask = np.array([float(p) in val_params for p in X[:, 1]], dtype=bool)
 542 |         train_idx = np.flatnonzero(~val_mask)
 543 |         val_idx = np.flatnonzero(val_mask)
 544 |         return X[train_idx], X[val_idx], y[train_idx], y[val_idx]
 545 | 
 546 |     train_indices: list[int] = []
 547 |     val_indices: list[int] = []
 548 |     for parameter in parameters:
 549 |         curve_idx = np.flatnonzero(X[:, 1] == parameter)
 550 |         ordered_idx = curve_idx[np.argsort(X[curve_idx, 0])]
 551 |         if len(ordered_idx) == 1:
 552 |             train_indices.extend(ordered_idx.tolist())
 553 |             val_indices.extend(ordered_idx.tolist())
 554 |             continue
 555 | 
 556 |         split = int(round(len(ordered_idx) * (1.0 - TEST_SIZE)))
 557 |         split = min(max(split, 1), len(ordered_idx) - 1)
 558 |         train_indices.extend(ordered_idx[:split].tolist())
 559 |         # Include the last training point so validation integration starts from
 560 |         # the known boundary state before predicting the held-out late segment.
 561 |         val_indices.extend(ordered_idx[split - 1 :].tolist())
 562 | 
 563 |     train_idx = np.sort(np.asarray(train_indices, dtype=int))
 564 |     val_idx = np.sort(np.asarray(val_indices, dtype=int))
 565 |     return X[train_idx], X[val_idx], y[train_idx], y[val_idx]
 566 | 
 567 | 
 568 | def load_dataset(data_file: Path) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]:
 569 |     """Load a single fit.tsv and return deterministic trajectory-preserving split."""
 570 |     X, y = _load_fit_table(data_file)
 571 |     return _trajectory_preserving_split(X, y)
 572 | 
 573 | 
 574 | def load_all_datasets(
 575 |     root: Path = DATA_ROOT,
 576 | ) -> list[tuple[str, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray, np.ndarray]]:
 577 |     """Load all datasets and return list of (name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val)."""
 578 |     entries = discover_datasets(root)
 579 |     results = []
 580 |     for name, path in entries:
 581 |         try:
 582 |             X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = load_dataset(path)
 583 |             results.append((name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val))
 584 |         except Exception as exc:
 585 |             print(f"Warning: skipping dataset {name}: {exc}")
 586 |     return results
 587 | 
 588 | 
 589 | NUM_WORKERS = int(os.environ.get("SKYDISCOVER_EVAL_WORKERS", min(8, os.cpu_count() or 4)))
 590 | PARSIMONY_WEIGHT = float(os.environ.get("SKYDISCOVER_PARSIMONY_WEIGHT", "0.20"))
 591 | PARSIMONY_COMPLEXITY_SCALE = float(
 592 |     os.environ.get("SKYDISCOVER_PARSIMONY_COMPLEXITY_SCALE", "160")
 593 | )
 594 | 
 595 | 
 596 | def _parsimony_penalty_factor(complexity: float) -> float:
 597 |     """Bounded penalty factor for expression complexity."""
 598 |     if not np.isfinite(complexity) or PARSIMONY_COMPLEXITY_SCALE <= 0:
 599 |         return 1.0
 600 |     normalized = min(max(float(complexity), 0.0) / PARSIMONY_COMPLEXITY_SCALE, 1.0)
 601 |     return max(0.0, 1.0 - PARSIMONY_WEIGHT * normalized)
 602 | 
 603 | 
 604 | def _gaussian_smooth_1d(values: np.ndarray, sigma: float = GAUSSIAN_SMOOTH_SIGMA) -> np.ndarray:
 605 |     values = np.asarray(values, dtype=float).ravel()
 606 |     if values.size < 3 or sigma <= 0.0:
 607 |         return values
 608 |     radius = max(1, int(round(3.0 * sigma)))
 609 |     offsets = np.arange(-radius, radius + 1, dtype=float)
 610 |     kernel = np.exp(-0.5 * (offsets / sigma) ** 2)
 611 |     kernel /= np.sum(kernel)
 612 |     padded = np.pad(values, (radius, radius), mode="edge")
 613 |     return np.convolve(padded, kernel, mode="valid")
 614 | 
 615 | 
 616 | def _curve_smoothed_target(X: np.ndarray, y_true: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
 617 |     """Smooth targets independently for each parameter curve in time order."""
 618 |     X = np.asarray(X, dtype=float)
 619 |     y_true = np.asarray(y_true, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 620 |     if X.shape[0] != y_true.shape[0] or X.shape[1] < 2:
 621 |         return _gaussian_smooth_1d(y_true)
 622 | 
 623 |     smoothed = np.empty_like(y_true, dtype=float)
 624 |     for parameter in np.unique(X[:, 1]):
 625 |         curve_idx = np.flatnonzero(X[:, 1] == parameter)
 626 |         ordered_idx = curve_idx[np.argsort(X[curve_idx, 0])]
 627 |         smoothed_curve = _gaussian_smooth_1d(y_true[ordered_idx])
 628 |         smoothed[ordered_idx] = smoothed_curve
 629 |     return smoothed
 630 | 
 631 | 
 632 | def _normalized_mse_with_reference_variance(
 633 |     y_true: np.ndarray,
 634 |     y_pred: np.ndarray,
 635 |     reference_values: np.ndarray,
 636 | ) -> float:
 637 |     """MSE normalized by full dataset scale, not by a flat validation segment."""
 638 |     y_true = np.asarray(y_true, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 639 |     y_pred = np.asarray(y_pred, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 640 |     reference_values = np.asarray(reference_values, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 641 |     if y_true.shape != y_pred.shape:
 642 |         return float("inf")
 643 |     if not np.all(np.isfinite(y_pred)):
 644 |         return float("inf")
 645 |     var = float(np.var(reference_values))
 646 |     if var <= 0.0:
 647 |         return float(np.mean((y_true - y_pred) ** 2))
 648 |     return float(np.mean((y_true - y_pred) ** 2) / var)
 649 | 
 650 | 
 651 | def _nmse_with_gaussian_smoothed_target(
 652 |     y_true: np.ndarray,
 653 |     y_pred: np.ndarray,
 654 |     X: np.ndarray | None = None,
 655 |     reference_values: np.ndarray | None = None,
 656 | ) -> float:
 657 |     reference = y_true if reference_values is None else reference_values
 658 |     raw_loss = _normalized_mse_with_reference_variance(y_true, y_pred, reference)
 659 |     smooth_target = _curve_smoothed_target(X, y_true) if X is not None else _gaussian_smooth_1d(y_true)
 660 |     smooth_loss = _normalized_mse_with_reference_variance(smooth_target, y_pred, reference)
 661 |     return float((raw_loss + smooth_loss) / 2.0)
 662 | 
 663 | 
 664 | def _ode_predictions(
 665 |     rhs_fn: Any,
 666 |     X: np.ndarray,
 667 |     y_known: np.ndarray,
 668 |     theta: np.ndarray,
 669 | ) -> np.ndarray:
 670 |     """Integrate dc/dt=f(t, parameter, c) per parameter curve on one split."""
 671 |     from scipy.integrate import odeint
 672 | 
 673 |     X = np.asarray(X, dtype=float)
 674 |     y_known = np.asarray(y_known, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 675 |     theta = np.asarray(theta, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 676 |     predictions = np.full_like(y_known, np.nan, dtype=float)
 677 | 
 678 |     for parameter in np.unique(X[:, 1]):
 679 |         curve_idx = np.flatnonzero(X[:, 1] == parameter)
 680 |         ordered_idx = curve_idx[np.argsort(X[curve_idx, 0])]
 681 |         times = X[ordered_idx, 0]
 682 |         observed = y_known[ordered_idx]
 683 | 
 684 |         if len(ordered_idx) == 1 or float(times[-1] - times[0]) <= 0.0:
 685 |             predictions[ordered_idx] = observed[0]
 686 |             continue
 687 | 
 688 |         def dc_dt(c_state: np.ndarray, time_value: float) -> float:
 689 |             c_value = float(np.asarray(c_state, dtype=float).reshape(-1)[0])
 690 |             value = rhs_fn(float(time_value), float(parameter), c_value, *theta)
 691 |             value = float(np.asarray(value, dtype=float).reshape(-1)[0])
 692 |             if not np.isfinite(value):
 693 |                 raise FloatingPointError("non-finite ODE derivative")
 694 |             return value
 695 | 
 696 |         integrated = odeint(
 697 |             dc_dt,
 698 |             float(observed[0]),
 699 |             times,
 700 |             mxstep=1000,
 701 |         )
 702 |         curve_pred = np.asarray(integrated, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 703 |         if curve_pred.shape != observed.shape or not np.all(np.isfinite(curve_pred)):
 704 |             raise FloatingPointError("non-finite ODE trajectory")
 705 |         predictions[ordered_idx] = curve_pred
 706 | 
 707 |     return predictions
 708 | 
 709 | 
 710 | def evaluate_ode_expression(
 711 |     expression: Any,
 712 |     X_train: np.ndarray,
 713 |     y_train: np.ndarray,
 714 |     X_val: np.ndarray,
 715 |     y_val: np.ndarray,
 716 |     *,
 717 |     constants: Any | None = None,
 718 |     initial_values: Any | None = None,
 719 |     max_nfev: int = 300,
 720 | ) -> dict[str, Any]:
 721 |     """Score a proposed ODE RHS by integrating predicted concentration curves."""
 722 |     import sympy as sp
 723 |     from pysr_harness import equation_session
 724 |     from scipy.optimize import least_squares
 725 | 
 726 |     X_train = np.asarray(X_train, dtype=float)
 727 |     y_train = np.asarray(y_train, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 728 |     X_val = np.asarray(X_val, dtype=float)
 729 |     y_val = np.asarray(y_val, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 730 |     y_reference = np.concatenate([y_train, y_val])
 731 |     feature_names = [f"x{i}" for i in range(X_train.shape[1])]
 732 |     template = equation_session._as_sympy_expr(expression, feature_names)
 733 |     equation_session._record_scored_template(template)
 734 | 
 735 |     if constants is None:
 736 |         feature_set = set(equation_session.feature_symbols(X_train.shape[1]))
 737 |         constants = sorted(template.free_symbols - feature_set, key=lambda s: s.name)
 738 |     constants = list(constants)
 739 |     equation_session._validate_expression_template(template, constants)
 740 | 
 741 |     if initial_values is None:
 742 |         base_x0 = np.ones(len(constants), dtype=float)
 743 |     else:
 744 |         base_x0 = np.asarray(initial_values, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
 745 |         if base_x0.size != len(constants):
 746 |             raise ValueError("initial_values must match the number of constants")
 747 | 
 748 |     feature_syms = equation_session.feature_symbols(X_train.shape[1])
 749 |     rhs_fn = sp.lambdify(feature_syms + constants, template, modules=["numpy"])
 750 | 
 751 |     if not constants:
 752 |         y_pred_train = _ode_predictions(rhs_fn, X_train, y_train, np.asarray([], dtype=float))
 753 |         y_pred_val = _ode_predictions(rhs_fn, X_val, y_val, np.asarray([], dtype=float))
 754 |         nmse_train = _nmse_with_gaussian_smoothed_target(
 755 |             y_train, y_pred_train, X_train, y_reference
 756 |         )
 757 |         nmse_val = _nmse_with_gaussian_smoothed_target(y_val, y_pred_val, X_val, y_reference)
 758 |         result = {
 759 |             "equation_template": f"d(c)/dt = {template}",
 760 |             "equation": f"d(c)/dt = {template}",
 761 |             "constants": {},
 762 |             "loss": float(np.mean((y_pred_train - y_train) ** 2)),
 763 |             "complexity": equation_session._complexity(template),
 764 |             "nmse_train": nmse_train,
 765 |             "nmse_val": nmse_val,
 766 |             "combined_score": combined_score_from_nmse(nmse_val),
 767 |         }
 768 |         equation_session._record_scorer_result(result)
 769 |         return result
 770 | 
 771 |     def residual(theta: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
 772 |         try:
 773 |             prediction = _ode_predictions(rhs_fn, X_train, y_train, theta)
 774 |         except Exception:
 775 |             return np.full_like(y_train, 1e12, dtype=float)
 776 |         if prediction.shape != y_train.shape or not np.all(np.isfinite(prediction)):
 777 |             return np.full_like(y_train, 1e12, dtype=float)
 778 |         return prediction - y_train
 779 | 
 780 |     initial_candidates: list[np.ndarray] = []
 781 |     for scale in ODE_MULTISTART_SCALES or (1.0,):
 782 |         candidate = base_x0 * float(scale)
 783 |         if not any(np.array_equal(candidate, existing) for existing in initial_candidates):
 784 |             initial_candidates.append(candidate)
 785 | 
 786 |     import warnings
 787 | 
 788 |     best_fit = None
 789 |     best_loss = float("inf")
 790 |     with warnings.catch_warnings(), np.errstate(over="ignore", divide="ignore", invalid="ignore"):
 791 |         warnings.simplefilter("ignore", RuntimeWarning)
 792 |         for initial_candidate in initial_candidates:
 793 |             fit = least_squares(
 794 |                 residual,
 795 |                 initial_candidate,
 796 |                 loss="soft_l1",
 797 |                 max_nfev=max_nfev,
 798 |             )
 799 |             fit_residual = residual(fit.x)
 800 |             fit_loss = float(np.mean(fit_residual**2))
 801 |             if fit_loss < best_loss:
 802 |                 best_loss = fit_loss
 803 |                 best_fit = fit
 804 | 
 805 |     if best_fit is None:
 806 |         raise RuntimeError("constant fitting did not produce a result")
 807 | 
 808 |     fitted_constants = {
 809 |         str(symbol): float(value) for symbol, value in zip(constants, best_fit.x)
 810 |     }
 811 |     substitutions = dict(zip(constants, best_fit.x))
 812 |     fitted_expr = template.subs(substitutions)
 813 |     y_pred_train = _ode_predictions(rhs_fn, X_train, y_train, best_fit.x)
 814 |     y_pred_val = _ode_predictions(rhs_fn, X_val, y_val, best_fit.x)
 815 |     nmse_train = _nmse_with_gaussian_smoothed_target(
 816 |         y_train, y_pred_train, X_train, y_reference
 817 |     )
 818 |     nmse_val = _nmse_with_gaussian_smoothed_target(y_val, y_pred_val, X_val, y_reference)
 819 |     loss = float(np.mean((y_pred_train - y_train) ** 2))
 820 | 
 821 |     result = {
 822 |         "equation_template": f"d(c)/dt = {template}",
 823 |         "equation": f"d(c)/dt = {fitted_expr}",
 824 |         "constants": fitted_constants,
 825 |         "loss": loss,
 826 |         "complexity": equation_session._complexity(template),
 827 |         "nmse_train": nmse_train,
 828 |         "nmse_val": nmse_val,
 829 |         "combined_score": combined_score_from_nmse(nmse_val),
 830 |     }
 831 |     equation_session._record_scorer_result(result)
 832 |     return result
 833 | 
 834 | 
 835 | def _aggregate_per_dataset_results(per_dataset_results: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, Any]:
 836 |     """Aggregate per-dataset metrics without letting failed datasets disappear."""
 837 |     raw_nmse_vals = [float(r["nmse_val"]) for r in per_dataset_results.values()]
 838 |     nmse_vals = [min(value, PER_DATASET_NMSE_CAP) for value in raw_nmse_vals]
 839 |     train_nmse_vals = [
 840 |         float(r["nmse_train"])
 841 |         for r in per_dataset_results.values()
 842 |         if np.isfinite(float(r.get("nmse_train", float("inf"))))
 843 |     ]
 844 |     complexity_vals = []
 845 |     for r in per_dataset_results.values():
 846 |         try:
 847 |             value = float(r.get("complexity", float("nan")))
 848 |         except (TypeError, ValueError):
 849 |             continue
 850 |         if np.isfinite(value):
 851 |             complexity_vals.append(value)
 852 |     aggregate_complexity = float(max(complexity_vals)) if complexity_vals else 0.0
 853 | 
 854 |     violation_errors = sorted(
 855 |         {
 856 |             str(r.get("error"))
 857 |             for r in per_dataset_results.values()
 858 |             if r.get("error") and SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX in str(r.get("error"))
 859 |         }
 860 |     )
 861 |     failed_datasets = sorted(
 862 |         name
 863 |         for name, result in per_dataset_results.items()
 864 |         if result.get("error") or not np.isfinite(float(result.get("nmse_val", float("inf"))))
 865 |     )
 866 |     templates = sorted(
 867 |         {
 868 |             str(r.get("equation_template"))
 869 |             for r in per_dataset_results.values()
 870 |             if r.get("combined_score", 0.0) > 0 and r.get("equation_template")
 871 |         }
 872 |     )
 873 | 
 874 |     eq_template = ""
 875 |     for r in per_dataset_results.values():
 876 |         if r.get("equation"):
 877 |             eq_template = r["equation"]
 878 |             break
 879 | 
 880 |     hard_cases = [
 881 |         {"dataset": name, "nmse_val": float(result["nmse_val"])}
 882 |         for name, result in per_dataset_results.items()
 883 |         if np.isfinite(float(result.get("nmse_val", float("inf"))))
 884 |     ]
 885 |     hard_cases.sort(key=lambda item: item["nmse_val"], reverse=True)
 886 |     hard_cases = hard_cases[:5]
 887 |     hard_case_feedback = "; ".join(
 888 |         f"{case['dataset']} nmse={case['nmse_val']:.4g}" for case in hard_cases
 889 |     )
 890 |     hard_case_tail_nmse = (
 891 |         float(np.mean([case["nmse_val"] for case in hard_cases[:HARD_CASE_TAIL_COUNT]]))
 892 |         if hard_cases and HARD_CASE_TAIL_COUNT > 0
 893 |         else 0.0
 894 |     )
 895 | 
 896 |     violation_error = None
 897 |     if violation_errors:
 898 |         violation_error = violation_errors[0]
 899 |     elif failed_datasets:
 900 |         examples = ", ".join(failed_datasets[:3])
 901 |         violation_error = (
 902 |             "Single-equation violation: candidate failed on "
 903 |             f"{len(failed_datasets)}/{len(per_dataset_results)} datasets. "
 904 |             "Failed datasets are not dropped from the aggregate reward. "
 905 |             f"Examples: {examples}"
 906 |         )
 907 |     elif len(templates) > 1:
 908 |         examples = "; ".join(templates[:3])
 909 |         violation_error = (
 910 |             "Single-equation violation: evaluate_symbolic_candidate() produced "
 911 |             f"{len(templates)} distinct equation templates across datasets. "
 912 |             "The base equation must be identical for all datasets; only fitted "
 913 |             f"constants may vary. Example templates: {examples}"
 914 |         )
 915 | 
 916 |     if violation_error:
 917 |         agg_nmse = float("inf")
 918 |         mean_nmse = float("inf")
 919 |         agg_train_nmse = float("inf")
 920 |         agg_combined = 0.0
 921 |         n_successful = 0
 922 |     else:
 923 |         mean_nmse = float(np.mean(nmse_vals)) if nmse_vals else float("inf")
 924 |         raw_mean_nmse = float(np.mean(raw_nmse_vals)) if raw_nmse_vals else float("inf")
 925 |         agg_nmse = mean_nmse + HARD_CASE_TAIL_WEIGHT * hard_case_tail_nmse
 926 |         agg_train_nmse = float(np.mean(train_nmse_vals)) if train_nmse_vals else float("inf")
 927 |         raw_combined = (
 928 |             float(1.0 / (1.0 + max(agg_nmse, 0.0))) if np.isfinite(agg_nmse) else 0.0
 929 |         )
 930 |         parsimony_factor = _parsimony_penalty_factor(aggregate_complexity)
 931 |         agg_combined = raw_combined * parsimony_factor
 932 |         n_successful = sum(1 for value in nmse_vals if np.isfinite(value))
 933 | 
 934 |     aggregated = {
 935 |         "equation": eq_template,
 936 |         "nmse_train": agg_train_nmse,
 937 |         "nmse_val": agg_nmse,
 938 |         "mean_nmse_val": mean_nmse,
 939 |         "mean_raw_nmse_val": raw_mean_nmse if not violation_error else float("inf"),
 940 |         "combined_score": agg_combined,
 941 |         "fit_score": raw_combined if not violation_error else 0.0,
 942 |         "complexity": aggregate_complexity,
 943 |         "parsimony_weight": PARSIMONY_WEIGHT,
 944 |         "parsimony_penalty_factor": (
 945 |             _parsimony_penalty_factor(aggregate_complexity) if not violation_error else 1.0
 946 |         ),
 947 |         "n_datasets": len(per_dataset_results),
 948 |         "n_successful": n_successful,
 949 |         "eval_workers": NUM_WORKERS,
 950 |         "hard_cases": hard_cases,
 951 |         "hard_case_feedback": hard_case_feedback,
 952 |         "hard_case_tail_nmse": hard_case_tail_nmse,
 953 |         "hard_case_tail_weight": HARD_CASE_TAIL_WEIGHT,
 954 |         "per_dataset_nmse_cap": PER_DATASET_NMSE_CAP,
 955 |         "per_dataset": per_dataset_results,
 956 |     }
 957 |     if violation_error:
 958 |         aggregated["error"] = violation_error
 959 |     return aggregated
 960 | 
 961 | 
 962 | def evaluate_candidate_with_timeout(program_path: str, timeout_seconds: int = 3000) -> dict:
 963 |     """Evaluate a candidate program across ALL datasets in a subprocess.
 964 | 
 965 |     The subprocess loads every dataset, calls the candidate's
 966 |     evaluate_symbolic_candidate() once per dataset (fitting independent
 967 |     constants each time) using parallel threads, and returns aggregated
 968 |     metrics.
 969 |     """
 970 |     validate_candidate_source(program_path)
 971 |     harness_root = str(BENCHMARK_ROOT)
 972 |     evaluator_path = str(Path(__file__).resolve())
 973 |     with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".py", delete=False) as temp_file:
 974 |         script = f"""
 975 | import importlib.util
 976 | import os
 977 | import pickle
 978 | import sys
 979 | import traceback
 980 | from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed
 981 | 
 982 | os.environ.setdefault("OMP_NUM_THREADS", "1")
 983 | os.environ.setdefault("MKL_NUM_THREADS", "1")
 984 | os.environ.setdefault("OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS", "1")
 985 | 
 986 | import numpy as np
 987 | 
 988 | sys.path.insert(0, {harness_root!r})
 989 | 
 990 | program_path = {program_path!r}
 991 | evaluator_path = {evaluator_path!r}
 992 | results_path = {temp_file.name + ".results"!r}
 993 | NUM_WORKERS = int(os.environ.get("SKYDISCOVER_EVAL_WORKERS", min(8, os.cpu_count() or 4)))
 994 | 
 995 | def _write_payload_and_exit(payload):
 996 |     with open(results_path, "wb") as f:
 997 |         pickle.dump(payload, f)
 998 |     sys.stdout.flush()
 999 |     sys.stderr.flush()
1000 |     # PySR/PythonCall can leave Julia runtime threads/finalizers alive at
1001 |     # interpreter shutdown. The parent only needs the pickle payload.
1002 |     os._exit(0)
1003 | 
1004 | try:
1005 |     evaluator_spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("benchmark_evaluator", evaluator_path)
1006 |     evaluator = importlib.util.module_from_spec(evaluator_spec)
1007 |     evaluator_spec.loader.exec_module(evaluator)
1008 |     evaluator.validate_candidate_source(program_path)
1009 | 
1010 |     import pysr_harness.equation_session as equation_session
1011 |     equation_session.evaluate_expression = evaluator.evaluate_ode_expression
1012 |     equation_session.nmse = evaluator._nmse_with_gaussian_smoothed_target
1013 |     spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("program", program_path)
1014 |     program = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
1015 |     spec.loader.exec_module(program)
1016 |     from pysr_harness.equation_session import (
1017 |         single_equation_evaluation,
1018 |         validate_single_equation_result,
1019 |     )
1020 | 
1021 |     fn = getattr(program, "evaluate_symbolic_candidate", None) or getattr(
1022 |         program, "run_discovery", None
1023 |     )
1024 |     if fn is None:
1025 |         raise AttributeError("Program must define evaluate_symbolic_candidate() or run_discovery()")
1026 | 
1027 |     datasets = evaluator.load_all_datasets()
1028 | 
1029 |     def _eval_one(args):
1030 |         ds_name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = args
1031 |         try:
1032 |             with single_equation_evaluation():
1033 |                 result = fn(X_train, y_train, X_val, y_val)
1034 |                 validate_single_equation_result(result)
1035 |             ds_nmse = float(result.get("nmse_val", float("inf")))
1036 |             ds_train_nmse = float(result.get("nmse_train", float("inf")))
1037 |             ds_combined = float(result.get("combined_score", 0.0))
1038 |             return ds_name, {{
1039 |                 "nmse_train": ds_train_nmse,
1040 |                 "nmse_val": ds_nmse,
1041 |                 "combined_score": ds_combined,
1042 |                 "complexity": float(result.get("complexity", 0.0)),
1043 |                 "equation": str(result.get("equation", "")),
1044 |                 "equation_template": str(result.get("equation_template", "")),
1045 |                 "constants": result.get("constants", {{}}),
1046 |             }}
1047 |         except Exception as exc:
1048 |             return ds_name, {{
1049 |                 "nmse_train": float("inf"),
1050 |                 "nmse_val": float("inf"),
1051 |                 "combined_score": 0.0,
1052 |                 "error": str(exc),
1053 |             }}
1054 | 
1055 |     per_dataset_results = {{}}
1056 |     with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=NUM_WORKERS) as executor:
1057 |         futures = [executor.submit(_eval_one, dataset) for dataset in datasets]
1058 |         for future in as_completed(futures):
1059 |             ds_name, ds_result = future.result()
1060 |             per_dataset_results[ds_name] = ds_result
1061 | 
1062 |     aggregated = evaluator._aggregate_per_dataset_results(per_dataset_results)
1063 | 
1064 |     _write_payload_and_exit({{"result": aggregated}})
1065 | except Exception as exc:
1066 |     traceback.print_exc()
1067 |     _write_payload_and_exit({{"error": str(exc)}})
1068 | """
1069 |         temp_file.write(script.encode())
1070 |         temp_path = temp_file.name
1071 | 
1072 |     results_path = f"{temp_path}.results"
1073 |     try:
1074 |         process = subprocess.Popen(
1075 |             [sys.executable, temp_path],
1076 |             stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
1077 |             stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
1078 |         )
1079 |         stdout, stderr = process.communicate(timeout=timeout_seconds)
1080 |         if stdout:
1081 |             print(stdout.decode())
1082 |         if stderr:
1083 |             print(stderr.decode())
1084 |         if process.returncode != 0:
1085 |             raise RuntimeError(f"Subprocess exited with code {process.returncode}")
1086 |         if not os.path.exists(results_path):
1087 |             raise RuntimeError("Results file not found")
1088 |         with open(results_path, "rb") as f:
1089 |             payload = pickle.load(f)
1090 |         if "error" in payload:
1091 |             raise RuntimeError(payload["error"])
1092 |         return payload["result"]
1093 |     except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
1094 |         process.kill()
1095 |         process.wait()
1096 |         raise TimeoutError(f"Program execution timed out after {timeout_seconds}s")
1097 |     finally:
1098 |         for path in (temp_path, results_path):
1099 |             if os.path.exists(path):
1100 |                 os.unlink(path)
1101 | 
1102 | 
1103 | def _metrics_from_result(result: dict, eval_time: float) -> dict:
1104 |     """Normalize aggregated harness output into SkyDiscover evaluator metrics."""
1105 |     nmse_val = float(result.get("nmse_val", float("inf")))
1106 |     combined = float(result.get("combined_score", combined_score_from_nmse(nmse_val)))
1107 |     if not np.isfinite(combined):
1108 |         combined = 0.0
1109 | 
1110 |     return {
1111 |         "equation": str(result.get("equation", "")),
1112 |         "loss": nmse_val,
1113 |         "complexity": float(result.get("complexity", 0.0)),
1114 |         "nmse_train": float(result.get("nmse_train", float("inf"))),
1115 |         "nmse_val": nmse_val,
1116 |         "mean_nmse_val": float(result.get("mean_nmse_val", nmse_val)),
1117 |         "mean_raw_nmse_val": float(result.get("mean_raw_nmse_val", nmse_val)),
1118 |         "combined_score": combined,
1119 |         "fit_score": float(result.get("fit_score", combined)),
1120 |         "parsimony_weight": float(result.get("parsimony_weight", PARSIMONY_WEIGHT)),
1121 |         "parsimony_penalty_factor": float(result.get("parsimony_penalty_factor", 1.0)),
1122 |         "eval_time": float(eval_time),
1123 |         "n_datasets": result.get("n_datasets", 0),
1124 |         "n_successful": result.get("n_successful", 0),
1125 |         "eval_workers": result.get("eval_workers", NUM_WORKERS),
1126 |         "hard_cases": result.get("hard_cases", []),
1127 |         "hard_case_feedback": str(result.get("hard_case_feedback", "")),
1128 |         "hard_case_tail_nmse": float(result.get("hard_case_tail_nmse", 0.0)),
1129 |         "hard_case_tail_weight": float(result.get("hard_case_tail_weight", HARD_CASE_TAIL_WEIGHT)),
1130 |         "per_dataset_nmse_cap": float(result.get("per_dataset_nmse_cap", PER_DATASET_NMSE_CAP)),
1131 |         "per_dataset": result.get("per_dataset", {}),
1132 |         "error": str(result.get("error", "")),
1133 |     }
1134 | 
1135 | 
1136 | def evaluate(program_path: str) -> dict:
1137 |     """Evaluate one evolved symbolic-equation candidate across all datasets."""
1138 |     try:
1139 |         start = time.time()
1140 |         result = evaluate_candidate_with_timeout(program_path, timeout_seconds=3000)
1141 |         metrics = _metrics_from_result(result, time.time() - start)
1142 |         print(
1143 |             "Evaluation: "
1144 |             f"nmse_val={metrics['nmse_val']:.6f}, "
1145 |             f"mean_nmse_val={metrics['mean_nmse_val']:.6f}, "
1146 |             f"mean_raw_nmse_val={metrics['mean_raw_nmse_val']:.6f}, "
1147 |             f"combined_score={metrics['combined_score']:.6f}, "
1148 |             f"n_datasets={metrics['n_datasets']}, "
1149 |             f"n_successful={metrics['n_successful']}, "
1150 |             f"time={metrics['eval_time']:.2f}s"
1151 |         )
1152 |         print(f"Equation template: {metrics['equation']}")
1153 |         if metrics.get("hard_case_feedback"):
1154 |             print(f"Hard-case feedback: {metrics['hard_case_feedback']}")
1155 |         if metrics.get("error"):
1156 |             print(f"Evaluator feedback: {metrics['error']}")
1157 |         return metrics
1158 |     except Exception as exc:
1159 |         print(f"Evaluation failed: {exc}")
1160 |         traceback.print_exc()
1161 |         return {
1162 |             "equation": "",
1163 |             "loss": float("inf"),
1164 |             "complexity": 0.0,
1165 |             "nmse_train": float("inf"),
1166 |             "nmse_val": float("inf"),
1167 |             "mean_nmse_val": float("inf"),
1168 |             "mean_raw_nmse_val": float("inf"),
1169 |             "combined_score": 0.0,
1170 |             "eval_time": 0.0,
1171 |             "n_datasets": 0,
1172 |             "n_successful": 0,
1173 |             "hard_cases": [],
1174 |             "hard_case_feedback": "",
1175 |             "hard_case_tail_nmse": 0.0,
1176 |             "hard_case_tail_weight": HARD_CASE_TAIL_WEIGHT,
1177 |             "per_dataset_nmse_cap": PER_DATASET_NMSE_CAP,
1178 |             "per_dataset": {},
1179 |             "error": str(exc),
1180 |         }
1181 | 
1182 | 
1183 | def _eval_stage1_worker(args):
1184 |     """Module-level worker for stage 1 parallel evaluation."""
1185 |     name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val, program_path = args
1186 |     harness_root = str(BENCHMARK_ROOT)
1187 |     if harness_root not in sys.path:
1188 |         sys.path.insert(0, harness_root)
1189 |     validate_candidate_source(program_path)
1190 |     import pysr_harness.equation_session as equation_session
1191 |     equation_session.evaluate_expression = evaluate_ode_expression
1192 |     equation_session.nmse = _nmse_with_gaussian_smoothed_target
1193 |     spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("program", program_path)
1194 |     program = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
1195 |     spec.loader.exec_module(program)
1196 |     from pysr_harness.equation_session import (
1197 |         single_equation_evaluation,
1198 |         validate_single_equation_result,
1199 |     )
1200 | 
1201 |     fn = getattr(program, "evaluate_symbolic_candidate", None) or getattr(
1202 |         program, "run_discovery", None
1203 |     )
1204 |     if fn is None:
1205 |         return 0.0, "missing evaluate_symbolic_candidate()", ""
1206 |     try:
1207 |         with single_equation_evaluation():
1208 |             result = fn(X_train, y_train, X_val, y_val)
1209 |             validate_single_equation_result(result)
1210 |         return float(result.get("combined_score", 0.0)), None, str(
1211 |             result.get("equation_template", "")
1212 |         )
1213 |     except Exception as exc:
1214 |         return 0.0, str(exc), ""
1215 | 
1216 | 
1217 | def evaluate_stage1(program_path: str) -> dict:
1218 |     """Fast cascade stage: evaluate on a small subset of datasets with reduced data."""
1219 |     try:
1220 |         harness_root = str(BENCHMARK_ROOT)
1221 |         if harness_root not in sys.path:
1222 |             sys.path.insert(0, harness_root)
1223 | 
1224 |         all_datasets = load_all_datasets()
1225 |         rng = np.random.default_rng(RANDOM_STATE)
1226 |         if len(all_datasets) > 8:
1227 |             indices = rng.choice(len(all_datasets), size=8, replace=False)
1228 |             subset = [all_datasets[i] for i in sorted(indices)]
1229 |         else:
1230 |             subset = all_datasets
1231 | 
1232 |         work_items = [
1233 |             (name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val, program_path)
1234 |             for name, X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val in subset
1235 |         ]
1236 | 
1237 |         with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=NUM_WORKERS) as executor:
1238 |             futures = [executor.submit(_eval_stage1_worker, item) for item in work_items]
1239 |             worker_results = [future.result() for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures)]
1240 | 
1241 |         combined_scores = [score for score, _error, _template in worker_results]
1242 |         successful_scores = [score for score in combined_scores if score > 0]
1243 |         errors = sorted({error for _score, error, _template in worker_results if error})
1244 |         violation_errors = sorted(
1245 |             {
1246 |                 error
1247 |                 for _score, error, _template in worker_results
1248 |                 if error and SINGLE_EQUATION_ERROR_PREFIX in error
1249 |             }
1250 |         )
1251 |         if violation_errors:
1252 |             return {
1253 |                 "combined_score": 0.0,
1254 |                 "n_stage1_datasets": len(subset),
1255 |                 "n_stage1_successful": len(successful_scores),
1256 |                 "eval_workers": NUM_WORKERS,
1257 |                 "error": violation_errors[0],
1258 |             }
1259 |         if errors:
1260 |             return {
1261 |                 "combined_score": 0.0,
1262 |                 "n_stage1_datasets": len(subset),
1263 |                 "n_stage1_successful": len(successful_scores),
1264 |                 "eval_workers": NUM_WORKERS,
1265 |                 "error": (
1266 |                     "Single-equation violation: stage 1 candidate failed on "
1267 |                     f"{len(errors)} dataset evaluations. Example: {errors[0]}"
1268 |                 ),
1269 |             }
1270 |         templates = sorted(
1271 |             {
1272 |                 template
1273 |                 for score, _error, template in worker_results
1274 |                 if score > 0 and template
1275 |             }
1276 |         )
1277 |         if len(templates) > 1:
1278 |             examples = "; ".join(templates[:3])
1279 |             return {
1280 |                 "combined_score": 0.0,
1281 |                 "n_stage1_datasets": len(subset),
1282 |                 "n_stage1_successful": len(successful_scores),
1283 |                 "eval_workers": NUM_WORKERS,
1284 |                 "error": (
1285 |                     "Single-equation violation: stage 1 produced "
1286 |                     f"{len(templates)} distinct equation templates across datasets. "
1287 |                     "The base equation must be identical for all datasets. "
1288 |                     f"Example templates: {examples}"
1289 |                 ),
1290 |             }
1291 |         agg_combined = float(np.mean(combined_scores)) if combined_scores else 0.0
1292 |         return {
1293 |             "combined_score": agg_combined,
1294 |             "n_stage1_datasets": len(subset),
1295 |             "n_stage1_successful": len(successful_scores),
1296 |             "eval_workers": NUM_WORKERS,
1297 |             "stage1_full_selected_datasets": 1.0,
1298 |         }
1299 |     except Exception as exc:
1300 |         print(f"Stage 1 evaluation failed: {exc}")
1301 |         return {"combined_score": 0.0, "error": str(exc)}
1302 | 
1303 | 
1304 | def evaluate_stage2(program_path: str) -> dict:
1305 |     """Full evaluation for cascade stage 2."""
1306 |     return evaluate(program_path)

import pandas as pd, numpy as np

# Look at a few representative datasets to understand kinetic shapes
files = [
    '/home/mrsar/protein-ag/past-published-data/disease-relevant non-inhibited/htt/Kar2011/Fits/Kar2011_Q37_sec/fit.tsv',
    '/home/mrsar/protein-ag/past-published-data/disease-relevant non-inhibited/Abeta/Cohen2013/Ab42_sec_IN_HOURS/fit.tsv',
    '/home/mrsar/protein-ag/past-published-data/disease-relevant non-inhibited/haemoglobin/Ferrone1985a/data and fits/sec_nuc_assuming_nc_n2_5/fit.tsv',
]
for f in files:
    df = pd.read_csv(f, sep='\t')
    name = f.split('/')[-2]
    t = df.iloc[:,0].values
    y = df.iloc[:,1:].values
    print(f'{name}: t=[{t.min():.2f},{t.max():.2f}], ncurves={y.shape[1]}')
    # Check shape: when does it plateau?
    for i in range(min(2, y.shape[1])):
        col = df.columns[i+1]
        yc = y[:,i]
        yc_valid = yc[~np.isnan(yc)]
        t10 = t[np.where(yc >= 0.1*yc_valid.max())[0][0]] if any(yc >= 0.1*yc_valid.max()) else np.nan
        t90 = t[np.where(yc >= 0.9*yc_valid.max())[0][0]] if any(yc >= 0.9*yc_valid.max()) else np.nan
        print(f'  curve {col[:30]}: t10={t10:.2f}, t90={t90:.2f}, max={yc_valid.max():.3f}')
